WHICH.Definition: (interrogative) What one or ones (of those mentioned or implied).

WOULD.Definition: Something that would happen, or would be the case, under different circumstances; a potentiality.

OTHER.Definition: An other, another (person, etc), more often rendered as another.

THESE.Definition: The thing, item, etc. being indicated.

COULD.Definition: (auxiliary verb, defective) To know how to; to be able to.

FIRST.Definition: The person or thing in the first position.

THOSE.Definition: (demonstrative) The thing, person, idea, quality, event, action or time indicated or understood from context, especially if more remote geographically, temporally or mentally than one designated as "this", or if expressing distinction.

WHERE.Definition: The place in which something happens.

AFTER.Definition: Later; second (of two); next, following, subsequent

YEARS.Definition: A solar year, the time it takes the Earth to complete one revolution of the Sun (between 365.24 and 365.26 days depending on the point of reference).

FOUND.Definition: To encounter or discover by accident; to happen upon.

WORLD.Definition: (with "the") Human collective existence; existence in general.

MIGHT.Definition: Power, strength, force or influence held by a person or group.

STATE.Definition: A condition; a set of circumstances applying at any given time.

STILL.Definition: A period of calm or silence.

WHILE.Definition: An uncertain duration of time, a period of time.

GREAT.Definition: A person of major significance, accomplishment or acclaim.

PLACE.Definition: (physical) An area; somewhere within an area.

GIVEN.Definition: (ditransitive) To move, shift, provide something abstract or concrete to someone or something or somewhere.

SMALL.Definition: Any part of something that is smaller or slimmer than the rest, now usually with anatomical reference to the back.

NEVER.Definition: At no time; on no occasion; in no circumstance.

POWER.Definition: Ability to do or undergo something.

LARGE.Definition: An old musical note, equal to two longas, four breves, or eight semibreves.

WATER.Definition: A substance (of molecular formula H2O) found at room temperature and pressure as a clear liquid; it is present naturally as rain, and found in rivers, lakes and seas; its solid form is ice and its gaseous form is steam.

GROUP.Definition: A number of things or persons being in some relation to one another.

SINCE.Definition: From a specified time in the past.

THINK.Definition: An act of thinking; consideration (of something).

AMONG.Definition: Denotes a mingling or intermixing with distinct or separable objects. (See Usage Note at amidst.)

LATER.Definition: Near the end of a period of time.

HUMAN.Definition: A human being, whether man, woman or child.

VALUE.Definition: The quality (positive or negative) that renders something desirable or valuable.

LEVEL.Definition: A tool for finding whether a surface is level, or for creating a horizontal or vertical line of reference.

UNTIL.Definition: Up to the time of (something happening).

EARLY.Definition: A shift (scheduled work period) that takes place early in the day.

STUDY.Definition: (usually academic) To review materials already learned in order to make sure one does not forget them, usually in preparation for an examination.

SHALL.Definition: (modal, auxiliary verb, defective) Used before a verb to indicate the simple future tense in the first person singular or plural.

KNOWN.Definition: To perceive the truth or factuality of; to be certain of or that.

BASED.Definition: To give as its foundation or starting point; to lay the foundation of.

LEAST.Definition: Something of the smallest possible extent; an indivisible unit.

WHOLE.Definition: Something complete, without any parts missing.

TAKEN.Definition: To get into one's hands, possession or control, with or without force.

WORDS.Definition: The smallest unit of language that has a particular meaning and can be expressed by itself; the smallest discrete, meaningful unit of language. (contrast morpheme.)

CHILD.Definition: A person who has not yet reached adulthood, whether natural (puberty), cultural (initiation), or legal (majority)

TIMES.Definition: The inevitable progression into the future with the passing of present and past events.

TERMS.Definition: That which limits the extent of anything; limit, extremity, bound, boundary.

SENSE.Definition: Any of the manners by which living beings perceive the physical world: for humans sight, smell, hearing, touch, taste.

PRESS.Definition: A device used to apply pressure to an item.

LOCAL.Definition: A person who lives near a given place.

LIGHT.Definition: Visible electromagnetic radiation. The human eye can typically detect radiation (light) in the wavelength range of about 400 to 750 nanometers. Nearby shorter and longer wavelength ranges, although not visible, are commonly called ultraviolet and infrared light.

YOUNG.Definition: People who are young; young people, collectively; youth.

HOUSE.Definition: A structure built or serving as an abode of human beings.

CASES.Definition: An actual event, situation, or fact.

ASKED.Definition: To request (information, or an answer to a question).

CLASS.Definition: A group, collection, category or set sharing characteristics or attributes.

MAJOR.Definition: A military rank between captain and lieutenant colonel.

DEATH.Definition: The cessation of life and all associated processes; the end of an organism's existence as an entity independent from its environment and its return to an inert, nonliving state

MODEL.Definition: A person who serves as a subject for artwork or fashion, usually in the medium of photography but also for painting or drawing.

SHOWN.Definition: To display, to have somebody see (something).

TOTAL.Definition: An amount obtained by the addition of smaller amounts.

WHOSE.Definition: (interrogative) That or those of whom or belonging to whom.

FIELD.Definition: A land area free of woodland, cities, and towns; open country.

BEGAN.Definition: To start, to initiate or take the first step into something.

WHITE.Definition: The color/colour of snow or milk; the colour of light containing equal amounts of all visible wavelengths.

MONEY.Definition: A legally or socially binding conceptual contract of entitlement to wealth, void of intrinsic value, payable for all debts and taxes, and regulated in supply.

AREAS.Definition: A measure of the extent of a surface; it is measured in square units.

CLEAR.Definition: Full extent; distance between extreme limits; especially; the distance between the nearest surfaces of two bodies, or the space between walls.

QUITE.Definition: (heading) To the greatest extent or degree; completely, entirely.

THING.Definition: That which is considered to exist as a separate entity, object, quality or concept.

NIGHT.Definition: The period between sunset and sunrise, when a location faces far away from the sun, thus when the sky is dark.

FORCE.Definition: Strength or energy of body or mind; active power; vigour; might; capacity of exercising an influence or producing an effect.

SEEMS.Definition: To appear; to look outwardly; to be perceived as.

TABLE.Definition: Furniture with a top surface to accommodate a variety of uses.

PARTY.Definition: A person or group of people constituting a particular side in a contract or legal action.

BLACK.Definition: The colour/color perceived in the absence of light, but also when no light is reflected, but rather absorbed.

CAUSE.Definition: (often with of, typically of adverse results) The source of, or reason for, an event or action; that which produces or effects a result.

LOWER.Definition: Situated close to, or even below, the ground or another normal reference plane; not high or lofty.

NEEDS.Definition: Of necessity; necessarily; indispensably; used with an auxiliary verb (often must), and equivalent to "of need".

SOUTH.Definition: One of the four major compass points, specifically 180�, directed toward the South Pole, and conventionally downwards on a map, abbreviated as S.

HEART.Definition: A muscular organ that pumps blood through the body, traditionally thought to be the seat of emotion.

THIRD.Definition: The person or thing in the third position.

RANGE.Definition: A line or series of mountains, buildings, etc.

STORY.Definition: A sequence of real or fictional events; or, an account of such a sequence.

TRADE.Definition: Buying and selling of goods and services on a market.

BLOOD.Definition: A member of the Los Angeles gang The Bloods.

HANDS.Definition: The part of the forelimb below the forearm or wrist in a human, and the corresponding part in many other animals.

COURT.Definition: An enclosed space; a courtyard; an uncovered area shut in by the walls of a building, or by different buildings; also, a space opening from a street and nearly surrounded by houses; a blind alley.

CELLS.Definition: A single-room dwelling for a hermit.

SHOWS.Definition: A play, dance, or other entertainment.

PAPER.Definition: A sheet material used for writing on or printing on (or as a non-waterproof container), usually made by draining cellulose fibres from a suspension in water.

PRICE.Definition: The cost required to gain possession of something.

TYPES.Definition: A grouping based on shared characteristics; a class.

HOURS.Definition: A time period of sixty minutes; one twenty-fourth of a day.

ISSUE.Definition: The action or an instance of flowing or coming out, an outflow, particularly:

NORTH.Definition: One of the four major compass points, specifically 0�, directed toward the North Pole, and conventionally upwards on a map, abbreviated as N.

LINES.Definition: A path through two or more points (compare �segment�); a continuous mark, including as made by a pen; any path, curved or straight.

BASIC.Definition: A necessary commodity, a staple requirement.

HEARD.Definition: (stative) To perceive sounds through the ear.

FRONT.Definition: The foremost side of something or the end that faces the direction it normally moves.

LEAVE.Definition: To have a consequence or remnant.

WROTE.Definition: To form letters, words or symbols on a surface in order to communicate.

VOICE.Definition: Sound uttered by the mouth, especially by human beings in speech or song; sound thus uttered considered as possessing some special quality or character

ADDED.Definition: To join or unite (e.g. one thing to another, or as several particulars) so as to increase the number, augment the quantity or enlarge the magnitude, or so as to form into one aggregate.

FINAL.Definition: A final examination; a test or examination given at the end of a term or class; the test that concludes a class.

COSTS.Definition: To incur a charge of; to require payment of a (specified) price.

IDEAS.Definition: An abstract archetype of a given thing, compared to which real-life examples are seen as imperfect approximations; pure essence, as opposed to actual examples.

BOOKS.Definition: A collection of sheets of paper bound together to hinge at one edge, containing printed or written material, pictures, etc.

MARCH.Definition: A formal, rhythmic way of walking, used especially by soldiers, bands and in ceremonies.

IMAGE.Definition: An optical or other representation of a real object; a graphic; a picture.

EQUAL.Definition: A person or thing of equal status to others.

TRUTH.Definition: True facts, genuine depiction or statements of reality.

MUSIC.Definition: A series of sounds organized in time, employing melody, harmony, tempo etc. usually to convey a mood.

COMES.Definition: To move from further away to nearer to.

LEGAL.Definition: The legal department of a company.

SCALE.Definition: A ladder; a series of steps; a means of ascending.

CHINA.Definition: The plant Smilax china, a liana of much of eastern Asia.

LABOR.Definition: Effort expended on a particular task; toil, work.

TRIED.Definition: Tested, hence, proven to be firm or reliable.

GIVES.Definition: (ditransitive) To move, shift, provide something abstract or concrete to someone or something or somewhere.

TAKES.Definition: The or an act of taking.

BUILT.Definition: To form (something) by combining materials or parts.

SHARE.Definition: A portion of something, especially a portion given or allotted to someone.

UNION.Definition: The act of uniting or joining two or more things into one.

NOTED.Definition: To notice with care; to observe; to remark; to heed.

MOVED.Definition: To change place or posture; to go, in any manner, from one place or position to another.

MORAL.Definition: (of a narrative) The ethical significance or practical lesson.

EARTH.Definition: The third planet of the Solar System; the world upon which humans live.

WRITE.Definition: The act or style of writing.

STAFF.Definition: (plural staffs or staves) A long, straight, thick wooden rod or stick, especially one used to assist in walking.

STYLE.Definition: Senses relating to a thin, pointed object.

ALLOW.Definition: To grant, give, admit, accord, afford, or yield; to let one have.

GOODS.Definition: The forces or behaviours that are the enemy of evil. Usually consists of helping others and general benevolence.

PLANT.Definition: An organism that is not an animal, especially an organism capable of photosynthesis. Typically a small or herbaceous organism of this kind, rather than a tree.

UNITS.Definition: A particular, minute unit of mass, defined differently for different substances, but so that varying substances of the same general type have the property that one international unit of the one has the same effect on the human body as one international unit of the other.

SPEAK.Definition: A low class bar, a speakeasy.

CROSS.Definition: A geometrical figure consisting of two straight lines or bars intersecting each other such that at least one of them is bisected by the other.

OFFER.Definition: A proposal that has been made.

PEACE.Definition: A state of tranquility, quiet, and harmony; absence of violence. For instance, a state free from civil disturbance.

CLAIM.Definition: A demand of ownership made for something.

STOOD.Definition: (heading) To position or be positioned physically.

LIVED.Definition: To be alive; to have life.

JAPAN.Definition: A hard black enamel varnish containing asphalt.

MILES.Definition: The international mile: a unit of length precisely equal to 1.609344 kilometers established by treaty among Anglophone nations in 1959, divided into 5,280 feet or 1,760 yards.

DOUBT.Definition: Disbelief or uncertainty (about something); a particular instance of such disbelief or uncertainty.

FAITH.Definition: A trust or confidence in the intentions or abilities of a person, object, or ideal from prior empirical evidence.

FULLY.Definition: In a full manner; without lack or defect.

SEVEN.Definition: The digit/figure 7 or an occurrence thereof.

WRONG.Definition: Something that is immoral or not good.

HENRY.Definition: In the International System of Units, the derived unit of electrical inductance; the inductance induced in a circuit by a rate of change of current of one ampere per second and a resulting electromotive force of one volt. Symbol: H

WEEKS.Definition: Any period of seven consecutive days.

ROUND.Definition: So as to form a circle or trace a circular path, or approximation thereof.

FOCUS.Definition: A point at which reflected or refracted rays of light converge.

FLOOR.Definition: The interior bottom or surface of a house or building; the supporting surface of a room.

HEAVY.Definition: A villain or bad guy; the one responsible for evil or aggressive acts.

NAMES.Definition: Any nounal word or phrase which indicates a particular person, place, class, or thing.

MONTH.Definition: A period into which a year is divided, historically based on the phases of the moon.

SMITH.Definition: A craftsperson who works metal into desired forms using a hammer and other tools, sometimes heating the metal to make it more workable, especially a blacksmith.

UPPER.Definition: A stimulant, such as amphetamine, that increases energy and decreases appetite.

COLOR.Definition: The spectral composition of visible light

FIXED.Definition: To pierce; now generally replaced by transfix.

EXIST.Definition: (stative) to be; have existence; have being or reality

OLDER.Definition: Of an object, concept, relationship, etc., having existed for a relatively long period of time.

AVOID.Definition: To try not to meet or communicate with (a person); to shun

REACH.Definition: The act of stretching or extending; extension.

BOARD.Definition: A relatively long, wide and thin piece of any material, usually wood or similar, often for use in construction or furniture-making.

CARRY.Definition: A manner of transporting or lifting something; the grip or position in which something is carried.

STEPS.Definition: An advance or movement made from one foot to the other; a pace.

DAILY.Definition: Something that is produced, consumed, used, or done every day.

SHAPE.Definition: The status or condition of something

RIVER.Definition: A large and often winding stream which drains a land mass, carrying water down from higher areas to a lower point, oftentimes ending in another body of water, such as an ocean or in an inland sea.

SPEED.Definition: The state of moving quickly or the capacity for rapid motion; rapidity.

RATIO.Definition: A number representing a comparison between two named things.

CHIEF.Definition: A leader or head of a group of people, organisation, etc.

CIVIL.Definition: Having to do with people and government office as opposed to the military or religion.

AWARE.Definition: Vigilant or on one's guard against danger or difficulty.

MEDIA.Definition: The middle layer of the wall of a blood vessel or lymph vessel which is composed of connective and muscular tissue.

BRAIN.Definition: The control center of the central nervous system of an animal located in the skull which is responsible for perception, cognition, attention, memory, emotion, and action.

TREES.Definition: Fast growing function based on Kruskal's tree theorem.

MOUTH.Definition: The opening of a creature through which food is ingested.

FACTS.Definition: Something actual as opposed to invented.

ERROR.Definition: The state, quality, or condition of being wrong.

TITLE.Definition: A prefix (honorific) or suffix (post-nominal) added to a person's name to signify either veneration, official position or a professional or academic qualification.

SCENE.Definition: The location of an event that attracts attention.

BIRTH.Definition: The process of childbearing; the beginning of life.

TRIAL.Definition: An opportunity to test something out; a test.

KINDS.Definition: A type, race or category; a group of entities that have common characteristics such that they may be grouped together.

GIRLS.Definition: A female child, adolescent, or a young woman.

TRUST.Definition: Confidence in or reliance on some person or quality.

ENTER.Definition: The "Enter" key on a computer keyboard.

ROMAN.Definition: One of the main three types used for the Latin alphabet (the others being italics and blackletter), in which the ascenders are mostly straight.

HAPPY.Definition: A happy event, thing, person, etc.

DRIVE.Definition: Motivation to do or achieve something; ability coupled with ambition.

CHECK.Definition: A situation in which the king is directly threatened by an opposing piece.

USUAL.Definition: The typical state of something, or something that is typical.

FUNDS.Definition: A sum or source of money.

SLEEP.Definition: To rest in a state of reduced consciousness.

BOUND.Definition: To tie; to confine by any ligature.

GREEK.Definition: An inhabitant, resident, or person of descent from Greece.

GOALS.Definition: A result that one is attempting to achieve.

BUILD.Definition: The physique of a human body; constitution or structure of a human body.

AGENT.Definition: One who exerts power, or has the power to act

BROAD.Definition: A shallow lake, one of a number of bodies of water in eastern Norfolk and Suffolk.

FORTH.Definition: Forward in time, place or degree.

RURAL.Definition: A person from the countryside; a rustic.

INPUT.Definition: The act or process of putting in; infusion.

PAGES.Definition: One of the many pieces of paper bound together within a book or similar document.

NOVEL.Definition: Newly made, formed or evolved; having no precedent; of recent origin; new.

JOINT.Definition: The point where two components of a structure join, but are still able to rotate.

ENEMY.Definition: Someone who is hostile to, feels hatred towards, opposes the interests of, or intends injury to someone else.

LIMIT.Definition: A restriction; a bound beyond which one may not go.

SPOKE.Definition: A support structure that connects the axle or the hub of a wheel to the rim.

LAYER.Definition: A single thickness of some material covering a surface.

SIDES.Definition: A bounding straight edge of a two-dimensional shape.

FIGHT.Definition: To contend in physical conflict, either singly or in war, battle etc.

WALLS.Definition: A rampart of earth, stones etc. built up for defensive purposes.

ADULT.Definition: A fully grown human or animal.

LEADS.Definition: A heavy, pliable, inelastic metal element, having a bright, bluish color, but easily tarnished; both malleable and ductile, though with little tenacity. It is easily fusible, forms alloys with other metals, and is an ingredient of solder and type metal. Atomic number 82, symbol Pb (from Latin plumbum).

CYCLE.Definition: An interval of space or time in which one set of events or phenomena is completed.

IDEAL.Definition: A perfect standard of beauty, intellect etc., or a standard of excellence to aim at.

BRIEF.Definition: A writ summoning one to answer to any action.

SIGNS.Definition: (sometimes also used uncountably) A visible indication.

BLOCK.Definition: A substantial, often approximately cuboid, piece of any substance.

INDEX.Definition: An alphabetical listing of items and their location.

BROWN.Definition: A colour like that of chocolate or coffee.

SOLID.Definition: A substance in the fundamental state of matter that retains its size and shape without need of a container (as opposed to a liquid or gas).

HORSE.Definition: Any of several animals related to Equus ferus caballus.

STORE.Definition: A place where items may be accumulated or routinely kept.

FRESH.Definition: A rush of water, along a river or onto the land; a flood.

SIGHT.Definition: (in the singular) The ability to see.

RAPID.Definition: (often in the plural) a rough section of a river or stream which is difficult to navigate due to the swift and turbulent motion of the water.

JUDGE.Definition: A public official whose duty it is to administer the law, especially by presiding over trials and rendering judgments; a justice.

CURVE.Definition: A gentle bend, such as in a road.

APART.Definition: (Used after a noun or in the predicate) Exceptional, distinct.

DEPTH.Definition: The vertical distance below a surface; the degree to which something is deep

CRIME.Definition: A specific act committed in violation of the law.

DRUGS.Definition: A substance used to treat an illness, relieve a symptom, or modify a chemical process in the body for a specific purpose.

FALSE.Definition: One of two options on a true-or-false test.

LOUIS.Definition: Any gold or silver coin issued by the French kings from Louis XIII to Louis XVI and bearing their image on the obverse side, particularly the gold louis d'ors, originally a French form of the Spanish doubloon but varying in value between 10 and 24 livres.

YOUTH.Definition: The quality or state of being young.

RADIO.Definition: The technology that allows for the transmission of sound or other signals by modulation of electromagnetic waves.

BANKS.Definition: An institution where one can place and borrow money and take care of financial affairs.

TOOLS.Definition: A mechanical device intended to make a task easier.

FRAME.Definition: The structural elements of a building or other constructed object.

STEEL.Definition: An artificial metal produced from iron, harder and more elastic than elemental iron; used figuratively as a symbol of hardness.

AHEAD.Definition: In or to the front; in advance; onward.

CHAIN.Definition: A series of interconnected rings or links usually made of metal.

GROWN.Definition: To become larger, to increase in magnitude.

MINOR.Definition: A person who is below the age of majority, consent, criminal responsibility or other adult responsibilities and accountabilities.

TASKS.Definition: A piece of work done as part of one�s duties.

ANGLE.Definition: A figure formed by two rays which start from a common point (a plane angle) or by three planes that intersect (a solid angle).

DREAM.Definition: Imaginary events seen in the mind while sleeping.

LOVED.Definition: (usually transitive, sometimes intransitive, stative) To have a strong affection for (someone or something).

YIELD.Definition: To pay, give in payment; repay, recompense; reward; requite.

FLUID.Definition: Any substance which can flow with relative ease, tends to assume the shape of its container, and obeys Bernoulli's principle; a liquid, gas or plasma.

RAISE.Definition: An increase in wages or salary; a rise (UK).

FIFTY.Definition: A banknote or coin with a denomination of 50.

TAXES.Definition: Money paid to the government other than for transaction-specific goods and services.

NOISE.Definition: Various sounds, usually unwanted or unpleasant.

UNITY.Definition: Oneness; the state or fact of being one undivided entity.

HENCE.Definition: From here, from this place, away

TRULY.Definition: (manner) In accordance with the facts; truthfully, accurately.

FORTY.Definition: A bottle of beer containing forty fluid ounces.

HEADS.Definition: The part of the body of an animal or human which contains the brain, mouth and main sense organs.

SMILE.Definition: A facial expression comprised by flexing the muscles of both ends of one's mouth, often showing the front teeth, without vocalisation, and in humans is a common involuntary or voluntary expression of happiness, pleasure, amusement or anxiety.

ROLES.Definition: A character or part played by a performer or actor.

FRANK.Definition: Free postage, a right exercised by governments (usually with definite article).

OWNED.Definition: To have rightful possession of (property, goods or capital); to have legal title to.

THEME.Definition: A subject of a talk or an artistic piece; a topic.

ROYAL.Definition: A royal person; a member of a royal family.

SCOPE.Definition: The breadth, depth or reach of a subject; a domain.

DRINK.Definition: To consume (a liquid) through the mouth.

TELLS.Definition: A reflexive, often habitual behavior, especially one occurring in a context that often features attempts at deception by persons under psychological stress (such as a poker game or police interrogation), that reveals information that the person exhibiting the behavior is attempting to withhold.

ENJOY.Definition: To receive pleasure or satisfaction from something

FINDS.Definition: Anything that is found (usually valuable), as objects on an archeological site or a person with talent.

COAST.Definition: The edge of the land where it meets an ocean, sea, gulf, bay, or large lake.

SPITE.Definition: Ill will or hatred toward another, accompanied with the desire to irritate, annoy, or thwart; a want to disturb or put out another; mild malice

SHIPS.Definition: A water-borne vessel generally larger than a boat.

SHARP.Definition: The symbol, placed after the name of a note in the key signature or before a note on the staff to indicate that the note is to be played a semitone higher.

FACED.Definition: (of a person or animal) To position oneself or itself so as to have one's face closest to (something).

TEXTS.Definition: A writing consisting of multiple glyphs, characters, symbols or sentences.

LANDS.Definition: The part of Earth which is not covered by oceans or other bodies of water.

SUGAR.Definition: Sucrose in the form of small crystals, obtained from sugar cane or sugar beet and used to sweeten food and drink.

WASTE.Definition: Excess of material, useless by-products or damaged, unsaleable products; garbage; rubbish.

BIRDS.Definition: A member of the class of animals Aves in the phylum Chordata, characterized by being warm-blooded, having feathers and wings usually capable of flight, and laying eggs.

QUICK.Definition: Raw or sensitive flesh, especially that underneath finger and toe nails.

NERVE.Definition: A bundle of neurons with their connective tissue sheaths, blood vessels and lymphatics.

FILES.Definition: A collection of papers collated and archived together.

TURNS.Definition: (heading) to make a non-linear physical movement.

FAVOR.Definition: A kind or helpful deed; an instance of voluntarily assisting (someone).

ROUTE.Definition: A course or way which is traveled or passed.

TASTE.Definition: One of the sensations produced by the tongue in response to certain chemicals; the quality of giving this sensation.

BROKE.Definition: To separate into two or more pieces, to fracture or crack, by a process that cannot easily be reversed for reassembly.

TEETH.Definition: The ability to be enforced, or to be enforced to any useful effect.

DANCE.Definition: A sequence of rhythmic steps or movements usually performed to music, for pleasure or as a form of social interaction.

FRUIT.Definition: (often in the plural) In general, a product of plant growth useful to man or animals.

SKILL.Definition: Capacity to do something well; technique, ability. Skills are usually acquired or learned, as opposed to abilities, which are often thought of as innate.

WAGES.Definition: (often in plural) An amount of money paid to a worker for a specified quantity of work, usually calculated on an hourly basis and expressed in an amount of money per hour.

WAVES.Definition: To relinquish (a right etc.); to give up claim to; to forego.

COUNT.Definition: The act of counting or tallying a quantity.

TEACH.Definition: To show (someone) the way; to guide, conduct; to point, indicate.

QUEEN.Definition: A female monarch. Example: Queen Victoria.

ACUTE.Definition: A person who has the acute form of a disorder, such as schizophrenia.

ARISE.Definition: To come up from a lower to a higher position.

ABUSE.Definition: Improper treatment or usage; application to a wrong or bad purpose; an unjust, corrupt or wrongful practice or custom.

CITED.Definition: To quote; to repeat, as a passage from a book, or the words of another.

SHEET.Definition: A thin bed cloth used as a covering for a mattress or as a layer over the sleeper.

TRACK.Definition: A mark left by something that has passed along.

ASIDE.Definition: An incidental remark made quietly so as to be heard by the person to whom it is said and not by any others in the vicinity.

VITAL.Definition: Relating to, or characteristic of life.

TOWNS.Definition: A settlement; an area with residential districts, shops and amenities, and its own local government; especially one larger than a village and smaller than a city.

HOMES.Definition: An informal term of address, like man or dude.

ARGUE.Definition: To show grounds for concluding (that); to indicate, imply.

ROOTS.Definition: The part of a plant, generally underground, that anchors and supports the plant body, absorbs and stores water and nutrients, and in some plants is able to perform vegetative reproduction.

ARMED.Definition: To take by the arm; to take up in one's arms.

DRESS.Definition: An item of clothing (usually worn by a woman or young girl) which both covers the upper part of the body and includes skirts below the waist.

LYING.Definition: To rest in a horizontal position on a surface.

MOVES.Definition: The act of moving; a movement.

FILMS.Definition: A thin layer of some substance; a pellicle; a membranous covering, causing opacity.

PRINT.Definition: Books and other material created by printing presses, considered collectively or as a medium.

HOPED.Definition: To want something to happen, with a sense of expectation that it might.

FACES.Definition: The front part of the head of a human or other animal, featuring the eyes, nose and mouth, and the surrounding area.

EXACT.Definition: To demand and enforce the payment or performance of, sometimes in a forcible or imperious way.

GRANT.Definition: The act of granting; a bestowing or conferring; concession; allowance; permission.

GRAIN.Definition: The harvested seeds of various grass food crops eg: wheat, corn, barley.

TREAT.Definition: An entertainment, outing, food, drink, or other indulgence provided by someone for the enjoyment of others.

CHEST.Definition: A box, now usually a large strong box with a secure convex lid.

STARS.Definition: Any small luminous dot appearing in the cloudless portion of the night sky, especially with a fixed location relative to other such dots.

LEWIS.Definition: A cramp iron inserted into a cavity in order to lift heavy stones; used as a symbol of strength in Freemasonry.

ANGER.Definition: A strong feeling of displeasure, hostility or antagonism towards someone or something, usually combined with an urge to harm.

SCORE.Definition: The total number of goals, points, runs, etc. earned by a participant in a game.

LIKED.Definition: To enjoy, be pleased by; favor; be in favor of.

TREND.Definition: An inclination in a particular direction.

ROCKS.Definition: An enormous mythical bird in Eastern legend.

DELAY.Definition: A period of time before an event occurs; the act of delaying; procrastination; lingering inactivity.

HELPS.Definition: Action given to provide assistance; aid.

SLAVE.Definition: A person who is held in servitude as the property of another person, and whose labor (and often also whose body and life) is subject to the owner's volition and control.

BONDS.Definition: Evidence of a long-term debt, by which the bond issuer (the borrower) is obliged to pay interest when due, and repay the principal at maturity, as specified on the face of the bond certificate. The rights of the holder are specified in the bond indenture, which contains the legal terms and conditions under which the bond was issued. Bonds are available in two forms: registered bonds, and bearer bonds.

CROWD.Definition: A group of people congregated or collected into a close body without order.

CATCH.Definition: The act of seizing or capturing.

TOPIC.Definition: Subject; theme; a category or general area of interest.

HOTEL.Definition: A large town house or mansion; a grand private residence, especially in France.

MATCH.Definition: A competitive sporting event such as a boxing meet, a baseball game, or a cricket match.

SWEET.Definition: The basic taste sensation induced by sugar.

NEGRO.Definition: A black person; a person of sub-Saharan African descent.

GRASS.Definition: Any plant of the family Poaceae, characterized by leaves that arise from nodes in the stem and leaf bases that wrap around the stem, especially those grown as ground cover rather than for grain.

GUESS.Definition: To reach a partly (or totally) unqualified conclusion.

NEWLY.Definition: Very recently; in the immediate past.

TEARS.Definition: A hole or break caused by tearing.

FALLS.Definition: The act of moving to a lower position under the effect of gravity.

LIVER.Definition: A large organ in the body that stores and metabolizes nutrients, destroys toxins and produces bile. It is responsible for thousands of biochemical reactions.

FIFTH.Definition: The person or thing in the fifth position.

FEELS.Definition: Of or relating to the emotions.

BREAD.Definition: A foodstuff made by baking dough made from cereals.

TENDS.Definition: (Old English law) To make a tender of; to offer or tender.

REPLY.Definition: A written or spoken response; part of a conversation.

SHOOK.Definition: A set of pieces for making a cask or box, usually wood.

LISTS.Definition: A strip of fabric, especially from the edge of a piece of cloth.

POEMS.Definition: A literary piece written in verse.

FAULT.Definition: A defect; something that detracts from perfection.

LINKS.Definition: Some text or a graphic in an electronic document that can be activated to display another document or trigger an action.

MALES.Definition: One of the male (masculine) sex or gender.

ESSAY.Definition: (authorship) A written composition of moderate length, exploring a particular issue or subject.

GUARD.Definition: A person who, or thing that, protects or watches over something.

ADMIT.Definition: To allow to enter; to grant entrance (to), whether into a place, into the mind, or into consideration

VIRUS.Definition: A submicroscopic, non-cellular structure consisting of a core of DNA or RNA surrounded by a protein coat, that requires a living host cell to replicate, and often causes disease in the host organism.

SOLVE.Definition: (chiefly law enforcement) A solution; an explanation.

THANK.Definition: An expression of appreciation; a thought.

DOORS.Definition: A portal of entry into a building, room, or vehicle, consisting of a rigid plane movable on a hinge. Doors are frequently made of wood or metal. May have a handle to help open and close, a latch to hold the door closed and a lock that ensures the door cannot be opened without the key.

SORRY.Definition: The act of saying sorry; an apology.

HONOR.Definition: Recognition of importance or value; respect; veneration (of someone, usually for being morally upright or successful)

PRIDE.Definition: The quality or state of being proud; an unreasonable overestimation of one's own superiority in terms of talents, looks, wealth, importance etc., which manifests itself in lofty airs, distance, reserve and often contempt of others.

CHOSE.Definition: To pick; to make the choice of; to select.

BLIND.Definition: A covering for a window to keep out light. The covering may be made of cloth or of narrow slats that can block light or allow it to pass.

FLOWS.Definition: A movement in people or things with a particular way in large numbers or amounts

WIDER.Definition: Having a large physical extent from side to side.

LOANS.Definition: An act or instance of lending, an act or instance of granting something for temporary use.

ROUGH.Definition: The unmowed part of a golf course.

RISKS.Definition: A possible adverse event or outcome

SONGS.Definition: A musical composition with lyrics for voice or voices, performed by singing.

FLESH.Definition: The soft tissue of the body, especially muscle and fat.

SMOKE.Definition: The visible vapor/vapour, gases, and fine particles given off by burning or smoldering material.

CROPS.Definition: A plant, especially a cereal, grown to be harvested as food, livestock fodder or fuel or for any other economic purpose.

GRAND.Definition: (plural "grand") A thousand of some unit of currency, such as dollars or pounds. (Compare G.)

CLICK.Definition: A brief, sharp, not particularly loud, relatively high-pitched sound produced by the impact of something small and hard against something hard, such as by the operation of a switch, a lock or a latch, or a finger pressed against the thumb and then released to strike the hand.

DROVE.Definition: A number of cattle driven to market or new pastures.

PRIME.Definition: The first hour of daylight; the first canonical hour.

ROADS.Definition: A way used for travelling between places, originally one wide enough to allow foot passengers and horses to travel, now (US) usually one surfaced with asphalt or concrete and designed to accommodate many vehicles travelling in both directions. In the UK both senses are heard: a country road is the same as a country lane.

DEALT.Definition: To distribute among a number of recipients, to give out as one�s portion or share.

GRAVE.Definition: An excavation in the earth as a place of burial

SAINT.Definition: A person whom a church or another religious group has officially recognised as especially holy or godly; one eminent for piety and virtue.

DRAMA.Definition: A composition, normally in prose, telling a story and intended to be represented by actors impersonating the characters and speaking the dialogue

OCEAN.Definition: One of the large bodies of water separating the continents.

VERSE.Definition: A poetic form with regular meter and a fixed rhyme scheme.

MODES.Definition: One of several ancient Greek scales.

DATED.Definition: To note the time or place of writing or executing; to express in an instrument the time of its execution.

BONES.Definition: A composite material consisting largely of calcium phosphate and collagen and making up the skeleton of most vertebrates.

SHELL.Definition: A hard external covering of an animal.

DYING.Definition: To stop living; to become dead; to undergo death.

ATOMS.Definition: The smallest possible amount of matter which still retains its identity as a chemical element, now known to consist of a nucleus surrounded by electrons.

DRAFT.Definition: A current of air, usually coming into a room or vehicle.

GENES.Definition: A theoretical unit of heredity of living organisms; a gene may take several values and in principle predetermines a precise trait of an organism's form (phenotype), such as hair color.

MARIA.Definition: A large, dark plain, which may have the appearance of a sea.

FOODS.Definition: Any solid substance that can be consumed by living organisms, especially by eating, in order to sustain life.

PANEL.Definition: A (usually) rectangular section of a surface, or of a covering or of a wall, fence etc.

STUFF.Definition: Miscellaneous items or objects; (with possessive) personal effects.

STICK.Definition: An elongated piece of wood or similar material, typically put to some use, for example as a wand or baton.

AIMED.Definition: To point or direct a missile, or a weapon which propels as missile, towards an object or spot with the intent of hitting it

PAIRS.Definition: Two similar or identical things taken together; often followed by of.

ADOPT.Definition: To take by choice into relationship (a child, heir, friend, citizen, etc.)

SHORE.Definition: Land adjoining a non-flowing body of water, such as an ocean, lake or pond.

ORGAN.Definition: A larger part of an organism, composed of tissues that perform similar functions.

HOPES.Definition: To want something to happen, with a sense of expectation that it might.

MOVIE.Definition: A recorded sequence of images displayed on a screen at a rate sufficiently fast to create the appearance of motion.

SLOPE.Definition: An area of ground that tends evenly upward or downward.

HABIT.Definition: An action performed on a regular basis.

DATES.Definition: The fruit of the date palm, Phoenix dactylifera, somewhat in the shape of an olive, containing a soft, sweet pulp and enclosing a hard kernel.

MARRY.Definition: To enter into the conjugal or connubial state; to take a husband or a wife.

PAINT.Definition: A substance that is applied as a liquid or paste, and dries into a solid coating that protects or adds color/colour to an object or surface to which it has been applied.

PROUD.Definition: Feeling honoured (by something); feeling happy or satisfied about an event or fact; gratified.

LUNCH.Definition: A light meal usually eaten around midday, notably when not as main meal of the day.

PULSE.Definition: A normally regular beat felt when arteries are depressed, caused by the pumping action of the heart.

CLOTH.Definition: A woven fabric such as used in dressing, decorating, cleaning or other practical use.

WOUND.Definition: An injury, such as a cut, stab, or tear, to a (usually external) part of the body.

GLORY.Definition: Great or overwhelming beauty or splendour.

STORM.Definition: Any disturbed state of the atmosphere, especially as affecting the earth's surface, and strongly implying destructive or unpleasant weather.

WHEEL.Definition: A circular device capable of rotating on its axis, facilitating movement or transportation or performing labour in machines.

NOBLE.Definition: An aristocrat; one of aristocratic blood.

TUMOR.Definition: An abnormal growth; differential diagnosis includes abscess, metaplasia, and neoplasia.

SIXTY.Definition: The cardinal number occurring after fifty-nine and before sixty-one, represented in Roman numerals as LX and in Arabic numerals as 60.

GIFTS.Definition: Something given to another voluntarily, without charge.

SEEDS.Definition: A fertilized and ripened ovule, containing an embryonic plant.

SHOES.Definition: A protective covering for the foot, with a bottom part composed of thick leather or plastic sole and often a thicker heel, and a softer upper part made of leather or synthetic material. Shoes generally do not extend above the ankle, as opposed to boots, which do.

ACRES.Definition: An English unit of land area (symbol: a. or ac.) originally denoting a day's plowing for a yoke of oxen, now standardized as 4,840 square yards or 4,046.86 square meters.

SHEEP.Definition: A woolly ruminant of the genus Ovis.

FEARS.Definition: A strong, uncontrollable, unpleasant emotion or feeling caused by actual or perceived danger or threat.

REALM.Definition: An abstract sphere of influence, real or imagined.

BASES.Definition: Something from which other things extend; a foundation.

NORMS.Definition: That which is normal or typical.

WHEAT.Definition: Any of several cereal grains, of the genus Triticum, that yields flour as used in bakery.

RULED.Definition: To regulate, be in charge of, make decisions for, reign over.

LAUGH.Definition: An expression of mirth particular to the human species; the sound heard in laughing; laughter.

BEACH.Definition: The shore of a body of water, especially when sandy or pebbly.

HILLS.Definition: An elevated location smaller than a mountain.

WIVES.Definition: A married woman, especially in relation to her spouse.

SERUM.Definition: The clear yellowish liquid obtained upon separating whole blood into its solid and liquid components after it has been allowed to clot.

FEVER.Definition: A higher than normal body temperature of a person (or, generally, a mammal), usually caused by disease.

STEAM.Definition: The vapor formed when water changes from liquid phase to gas phase.

MAGIC.Definition: The application of rituals or actions, especially those based on occult knowledge, to subdue or manipulate natural or supernatural beings and forces in order to have some benefit from them

ELITE.Definition: A special group or social class of people which have a superior intellectual, social or economic status as, the elite of society.

HOLES.Definition: A hollow place or cavity; an excavation; a pit; an opening in or through a solid body, a fabric, etc.; a perforation; a rent; a fissure.

DEALS.Definition: A division, a portion, a share.

BANDS.Definition: A strip of material used for strengthening or coupling.

FILED.Definition: To commit (official papers) to some office.

USAGE.Definition: The manner or the amount of using; use.

TEAMS.Definition: A set of draught animals, such as two horses in front of a carriage.

ALIKE.Definition: Having resemblance or similitude; similar; without difference.

LABEL.Definition: A small ticket or sign giving information about something to which it is attached or intended to be attached.

ANGLO.Definition: An English person or person of English ancestry.

YARDS.Definition: A small, usually uncultivated area adjoining or (now especially) within the precincts of a house or other building.

FIRED.Definition: To set (something, often a building) on fire.

ALTER.Definition: To change the form or structure of.

EDGES.Definition: The boundary line of a surface.

CABLE.Definition: (material) A long object used to make a physical connection.

URGED.Definition: To press; to push; to drive; to impel; to force onward.

AROSE.Definition: To come up from a lower to a higher position.

PILOT.Definition: A person who steers a ship, a helmsman.

TRUCK.Definition: A small wheel or roller, specifically the wheel of a gun carriage.

SOLAR.Definition: Of or pertaining to the sun; proceeding from the sun

RIGID.Definition: A bicycle with no suspension system.

SMELL.Definition: A sensation, pleasant or unpleasant, detected by inhaling air (or, the case of water-breathing animals, water) carrying airborne molecules of a substance.

CLOCK.Definition: An instrument used to measure or keep track of time; a non-portable timepiece.

ASSET.Definition: Something or someone of any value; any portion of one's property or effects so considered.

KEEPS.Definition: The main tower of a castle or fortress, located within the castle walls.

FIBER.Definition: A single elongated piece of a given material, roughly round in cross-section, often twisted with other fibers to form thread.

ACTOR.Definition: A person who performs, plays a part in a theatrical play or film.

RANKS.Definition: A row of people or things organized in a grid pattern, often soldiers [the corresponding term for the perpendicular columns in such a pattern is "file"].

VOTES.Definition: A formalized choice on matters of administration or other democratic activities

FARMS.Definition: A place where agricultural and similar activities take place, especially the growing of crops or the raising of livestock.

DRIED.Definition: Without water or moisture, said of something that has previously been wet or moist; resulting from the process of drying.

SEEKS.Definition: To try to find; to look for; to search for.

VALVE.Definition: A device that controls the flow of a gas or fluid through a pipe.

MOUSE.Definition: Any small rodent of the genus Mus.

LASER.Definition: A device that produces a monochromatic, coherent beam of light.

SHIRT.Definition: An article of clothing that is worn on the upper part of the body, and often has sleeves, either long or short, that cover the arms.

CREAM.Definition: The butterfat/milkfat part of milk which rises to the top; this part when separated from the remainder.

GIANT.Definition: A mythical human of very great size.

KNEES.Definition: In humans, the joint or the region of the joint in the middle part of the leg between the thigh and the shank.

TRANS.Definition: To cross from one side to another of (gender, sex or something in that vein).

CREEK.Definition: A small inlet or bay, often saltwater, narrower and extending farther into the land than a cove; a recess in the shore of the sea, or of a river; the inner part of a port that is used as a dock for small boats.

BOXES.Definition: Senses relating to a three-dimensional object or space.

CROWN.Definition: A royal, imperial or princely headdress; a diadem.

IMPLY.Definition: (of a proposition) to have as a necessary consequence

GRAPH.Definition: (applied mathematics) A data chart (graphical representation of data) intended to illustrate the relationship between a set (or sets) of numbers (quantities, measurements or indicative numbers) and a reference set, whose elements are indexed to those of the former set(s) and may or may not be numbers.

BEARS.Definition: A large omnivorous mammal, related to the dog and raccoon, having shaggy hair, a very small tail, and flat feet; a member of family Ursidae.

ZONES.Definition: Each of the five regions of the earth's surface into which it was divided by climatic differences, namely the torrid zone (between the tropics), two temperate zones (between the tropics and the polar circles), and two frigid zones (within the polar circles).

WINGS.Definition: An appendage of an animal's (bird, bat, insect) body that enables it to fly; a similar fin at the side of a ray or similar fish

TRAIL.Definition: The track or indication marking the route followed by something that has passed, such as the footprints of animal on land or the contrail of an airplane in the sky.

KNIFE.Definition: A utensil or a tool designed for cutting, consisting of a flat piece of hard material, usually steel or other metal (the blade), usually sharpened on one edge, attached to a handle. The blade may be pointed for piercing.

NODES.Definition: A knot, knob, protuberance or swelling.

WOODS.Definition: The substance making up the central part of the trunk and branches of a tree. Used as a material for construction, to manufacture various items, etc. or as fuel.

CODES.Definition: A short symbol, often with little relation to the item it represents.

ONSET.Definition: An attack; an assault especially of an army.

SOILS.Definition: A mixture of mineral particles and organic material, used to support plant growth.

BOATS.Definition: A craft used for transportation of goods, fishing, racing, recreational cruising, or military use on or in the water, propelled by oars or outboard motor or inboard motor or by wind.

BILLS.Definition: Any of various bladed or pointed hand weapons, originally designating an Anglo-Saxon sword, and later a weapon of infantry, especially in the 14th and 15th centuries, commonly consisting of a broad, heavy, double-edged, hook-shaped blade, with a short pike at the back and another at the top, attached to the end of a long staff.

BURST.Definition: An act or instance of bursting.

SIXTH.Definition: (not used in the plural) The person or thing in the sixth position.

ROGER.Definition: (radio telecommunications) Received (used in radio communications to acknowledge that a message has been received and understood)

NAVAL.Definition: Of or relating to a navy.

GROWS.Definition: To become larger, to increase in magnitude.

SWORD.Definition: A long-bladed weapon with a hilt, and usually a pommel and cross-guard, which is designed to stab, slash, and/or hack.

SUPRA.Definition: Used to indicate that the current citation is from the same source as the previous one.

TALKS.Definition: To communicate, usually by means of speech.

PITCH.Definition: A sticky, gummy substance secreted by trees; sap.

YOURS.Definition: That which belongs to you (singular); the possessive second-person singular pronoun used without a following noun.

HIRED.Definition: To obtain the services of in return for fixed payment.

TRIES.Definition: An ordered tree data structure that is used to store an associative array where the keys are usually strings.

PATHS.Definition: A trail for the use of, or worn by, pedestrians.

SOULS.Definition: The spirit or essence of a person usually thought to consist of one's thoughts and personality. Often believed to live on after the person's death.

SHOPS.Definition: An establishment that sells goods or services to the public; originally only a physical location, but now a virtual establishment as well.

CORPS.Definition: A battlefield formation composed of two or more divisions.

RISES.Definition: To move, or appear to move, physically upwards relative to the ground.

MIDST.Definition: A place in the middle of something; may be used of a literal or metaphorical location.

MERIT.Definition: A claim to commendation or a reward.

STUCK.Definition: To become or remain attached; to adhere.

LOVER.Definition: One who loves and cares for another person in a romantic way; a sweetheart, love, soulmate, boyfriend, girlfriend or spouse.

OWING.Definition: To be under an obligation to give something back to someone or to perform some action for someone.

CANAL.Definition: An artificial waterway or artificially improved river used for travel, shipping, or irrigation.

DECAY.Definition: The process or result of being gradually decomposed.

UNCLE.Definition: The brother or brother-in-law of one�s parent.

URINE.Definition: Liquid waste consisting of water, salts and urea, which is made in the kidneys, stored in the bladder, then released through the urethra.

KINGS.Definition: A male monarch; a man who heads a monarchy. If it's an absolute monarchy, then he is the supreme ruler of his nation.

BAKER.Definition: A person who bakes and sells bread, cakes and similar items.

OPENS.Definition: To make something accessible or allow for passage by moving from a shut position.

TRIBE.Definition: A socially, ethnically, or politically cohesive group of people.

SPORT.Definition: Any activity that uses physical exertion or skills competitively under a set of rules that is not based on aesthetics.

BLANK.Definition: A small French coin, originally of silver, afterwards of copper, worth 5 deniers; also a silver coin of Henry V current in the parts of France then held by the English, worth about 8 pence .

TOUGH.Definition: A person who obtains things by force; a thug or bully.

FLEET.Definition: A group of vessels or vehicles.

FLOOD.Definition: A (usually disastrous) overflow of water from a lake or other body of water due to excessive rainfall or other input of water.

TOWER.Definition: A very tall iron-framed structure, usually painted red and white, on which microwave, radio, satellite, or other communication antennas are installed; mast.

STRIP.Definition: A long, thin piece of land; any long, thin area.

READS.Definition: A reading or an act of reading, especially an actor's part of a play.

ALIEN.Definition: Any life form of extraterrestrial or extradimensional origin.

PIANO.Definition: A percussive keyboard musical instrument, usually ranging over seven octaves, with white and black colored keys, played by pressing these keys, causing hammers to strike strings

POUND.Definition: A unit of mass equal to 16 avoirdupois ounces (= 453.592 37 g). Today this value is the most common meaning of "pound" as a unit of weight.

TUBES.Definition: Anything that is hollow and cylindrical in shape.

SHAME.Definition: Uncomfortable or painful feeling due to recognition or consciousness of one's own impropriety or dishonor or something being exposed that should have been kept private.

SHOOT.Definition: The emerging stem and embryonic leaves of a new plant.

CRUDE.Definition: Any substance in its natural state.

AWARD.Definition: A judgment, sentence, or final decision. Specifically: The decision of arbitrators in a case submitted.

BUYER.Definition: A person who makes one or more purchases.

BORNE.Definition: To carry or convey, literally or figuratively.

GUEST.Definition: A recipient of hospitality, specifically someone staying by invitation at the house of another.

CAMPS.Definition: An outdoor place acting as temporary accommodation in tents or other temporary structures.

CRACK.Definition: A thin and usually jagged space opened in a previously solid material.

RACES.Definition: A contest between people, animals, vehicles, etc. where the goal is to be the first to reach some objective. Example: Several horses run in a horse race, and the first one to reach the finishing post wins

OPERA.Definition: A theatrical work, combining drama, music, song and sometimes dance.

DROPS.Definition: A small quantity of liquid, just large enough to hold its own round shape via surface tension, especially one that falls from a source of liquid.

BRUSH.Definition: An implement consisting of multiple more or less flexible bristles or other filaments attached to a handle, used for any of various purposes including cleaning, painting, and arranging hair.

PUPIL.Definition: A learner under the supervision of a teacher or professor.

MERCY.Definition: Relenting; forbearance to cause or allow harm to another.

RULER.Definition: A (usually rigid), flat, rectangular measuring or drawing device with graduations in units of measurement; a straightedge with markings.

SWEPT.Definition: To clean (a surface) by means of a stroking motion of a broom or brush.

WINDS.Definition: Real or perceived movement of atmospheric air usually caused by convection or differences in air pressure.

TALES.Definition: Any of several units of measure used in China and elsewhere in eastern Asia, approximately 40 grams.

SPOTS.Definition: A round or irregular patch on the surface of a thing having a different color, texture etc. and generally round in shape.

OXIDE.Definition: A binary chemical compound of oxygen with another chemical element.

POSTS.Definition: A long dowel or plank protruding from the ground; a fencepost; a lightpost.

REACT.Definition: An emoji used to express a reaction to a post on social media.

FATAL.Definition: A fatality; an event that leads to death.

COHEN.Definition: A Jewish priest: direct male descendant of the Biblical high priest Aaron, brother of Moses.

RIVAL.Definition: A competitor (person, team, company, etc.) with the same goal as another, or striving to attain the same thing. Defeating a rival may be a primary or necessary goal of a competitor.

SLIDE.Definition: An item of play equipment that children can climb up and then slide down again.

BRICK.Definition: A hardened rectangular block of mud, clay etc., used for building.

JUICE.Definition: A liquid from a plant, especially fruit.

MEALS.Definition: Food that is prepared and eaten, usually at a specific time, and usually in a comparatively large quantity (as opposed to a snack).

PORTS.Definition: A place on the coast at which ships can shelter, or dock to load and unload cargo or passengers.

NANCY.Definition: An effeminate man, especially a homosexual.

RINGS.Definition: (physical) A solid object in the shape of a circle.

FLAME.Definition: The visible part of fire; a stream of burning vapour or gas, emitting light and heat.

MINES.Definition: Demeanor; facial expression or attitude, especially one which is intended by its bearer.

DRAWS.Definition: The result of a contest that neither side has won; a tie.

SHADE.Definition: Darkness where light, particularly sunlight, is blocked.

TOXIC.Definition: Having a chemical nature that is harmful to health or lethal if consumed or otherwise entering into the body in sufficient quantities.

PROSE.Definition: Language, particularly written language, not intended as poetry.

SHEAR.Definition: A cutting tool similar to scissors, but often larger.

CLUBS.Definition: An association of members joining together for some common purpose, especially sports or recreation.

HUMOR.Definition: The quality of being amusing, comical, funny.

CRAZY.Definition: An insane or eccentric person; a crackpot.

MEETS.Definition: A sports competition, especially for track and field (a track meet) or swimming (a swim meet).

GASES.Definition: Matter in a state intermediate between liquid and plasma that can be contained only if it is fully surrounded by a solid (or in a bubble of liquid) (or held together by gravitational pull); it can condense into a liquid, or can (rarely) become a solid directly.

TANKS.Definition: A closed container for liquids or gases.

AGING.Definition: To cause to grow old; to impart the characteristics of age to.

ARROW.Definition: A projectile consisting of a shaft, a point and a tail with stabilizing fins that is shot from a bow.

BASIN.Definition: A wide bowl for washing, sometimes affixed to a wall

SIZED.Definition: To adjust the size of; to make a certain size.

CLERK.Definition: One who occupationally works with records, accounts, letters, etc.; an office worker.

SPARE.Definition: The act of sparing; moderation; restraint.

STOPS.Definition: A (usually marked) place where buses, trams or trains halt to let passengers get on and off, usually smaller than a station.

DRUNK.Definition: To consume (a liquid) through the mouth.

CASTE.Definition: Any of the hereditary social classes and subclasses of South Asian societies.

LUCKY.Definition: (of people) Favoured by luck; fortunate; having good success or good fortune

RESTS.Definition: (of a person or animal) Relief from work or activity by sleeping; sleep.

ALARM.Definition: A summons to arms, as on the approach of an enemy.

POSED.Definition: To place in an attitude or fixed position, for the sake of effect.

VOTED.Definition: To cast a vote; to assert a formalized choice in an election

CABIN.Definition: A small dwelling characteristic of the frontier, especially when built from logs with simple tools and not constructed by professional builders, but by those who meant to live in it.

LINED.Definition: To place (objects) into a line (usually used with "up"); to form into a line; to align.

FLOUR.Definition: Powder obtained by grinding or milling cereal grains, especially wheat, or other foodstuffs such as soybeans and potatoes, and used to bake bread, cakes, and pastry.

CRUEL.Definition: To spoil or ruin (one's chance of success)

QUOTE.Definition: A quotation; a statement attributed to a person.

SLEPT.Definition: To rest in a state of reduced consciousness.

PLOTS.Definition: (authorship) The course of a story, comprising a series of incidents which are gradually unfolded, sometimes by unexpected means.

DEBTS.Definition: An action, state of mind, or object one has an obligation to perform for another, adopt toward another, or give to another.

SHAFT.Definition: The entire body of a long weapon, such as an arrow.

WIDOW.Definition: A woman whose spouse has died (and who has not remarried); feminine of widower.

COACH.Definition: A wheeled vehicle, generally drawn by horse power.

TRIPS.Definition: A journey; an excursion or jaunt

STAKE.Definition: A piece of wood or other material, usually long and slender, pointed at one end so as to be easily driven into the ground as a marker or a support or stay.

STEEP.Definition: The steep side of a mountain etc.; a slope or acclivity.

LUNGS.Definition: A biological organ of vertebrates that controls breathing and oxygenates the blood.

LIKES.Definition: (usually in the plural) Something that a person likes (prefers).

PANIC.Definition: Overpowering fright, often affecting groups of people or animals.

LOADS.Definition: A burden; a weight to be carried.

CENTS.Definition: (money) A subunit of currency equal to one-hundredth of the main unit of currency in many countries. Symbol: �.

PAUSE.Definition: A button whose functions are pausing and resuming something, such as a DVD player, a video game or a computer.

GATES.Definition: A doorlike structure outside a house.

VEINS.Definition: A blood vessel that transports blood from the capillaries back to the heart.

WEBER.Definition: In the International System of Units, the derived unit of magnetic flux; the flux linking a circuit of one turn that produces an electromotive force of one volt when reduced uniformly to zero in one second. Symbol: Wb.

HAVEN.Definition: A harbour or anchorage protected from the sea.

TOOTH.Definition: A hard, calcareous structure present in the mouth of many vertebrate animals, generally used for eating.

LOYAL.Definition: Having or demonstrating undivided and constant support for someone or something.

EXILE.Definition: The state of being banished from one's home or country.

GENRE.Definition: A kind; a stylistic category or sort, especially of literature or other artworks.

STEMS.Definition: Acronym of science, technology, engineering, (and) mathematics.

FIRES.Definition: A (usually self-sustaining) chemical reaction involving the bonding of oxygen with carbon or other fuel, with the production of heat and the presence of flame or smouldering.

FENCE.Definition: A thin artificial barrier that separates two pieces of land or a house perimeter.

METER.Definition: (always meter) A device that measures things.

DIARY.Definition: A daily log of experiences, especially those of the writer.

TENSE.Definition: (grammar) Any of the forms of a verb which distinguish when an action or state of being occurs or exists.

QUEST.Definition: A journey or effort in pursuit of a goal (often lengthy, ambitious, or fervent); a mission.

DEEDS.Definition: An action or act; something that is done.

FLASH.Definition: A device that produces a short flash of light to help illuminate a scene, mostly for night-time or indoors photography.

SWING.Definition: The manner in which something is swung.

PROBE.Definition: Any of various medical instruments used to explore wounds, organs, etc.

SHAKE.Definition: The act of shaking or being shaken; tremulous or back-and-forth motion.

CHAOS.Definition: The unordered state of matter in classical accounts of cosmogony.

SPINE.Definition: The series of bones situated at the back from the head to the pelvis of a person, or from the head to the tail of an animal; backbone, vertebral column.

BLADE.Definition: The sharp cutting edge of a knife, chisel, or other tool, a razor blade/sword.

BRIDE.Definition: A woman in the context of her own wedding; one who is going to marry or has just been married.

DRIFT.Definition: (physical) Movement; that which moves or is moved.

CHEEK.Definition: The soft skin on each side of the face, below the eyes; the outer surface of the sides of the oral cavity.

GLAND.Definition: An organ that synthesizes a substance, such as hormones or breast milk, and releases it, often into the bloodstream (endocrine gland) or into cavities inside the body or its outer surface (exocrine gland).

CHILE.Definition: The pungent, spicy fresh or dried fruit of any of several cultivated varieties of capsicum peppers, used in cooking.

PEAKS.Definition: A point; the sharp end or top of anything that terminates in a point; as, the peak, or front, of a cap.

FOCAL.Definition: Belonging to, concerning, or located at a focus

SPELL.Definition: Words or a formula supposed to have magical powers.

FRAUD.Definition: The crime of stealing or otherwise illegally obtaining money by use of deception tactics.

WALKS.Definition: To move on the feet by alternately setting each foot (or pair or group of feet, in the case of animals with four or more feet) forward, with at least one foot on the ground at all times. Compare run.

BENCH.Definition: A long seat with or without a back, found for example in parks and schools.

ALTAR.Definition: A table or similar flat-topped structure used for religious rites.

CARGO.Definition: Freight carried by a ship, aircraft, or motor vehicle.

THUMB.Definition: The short thick digit of the hand that for humans has the most mobility and can be made to oppose (moved to touch) all of the other fingers.

ORBIT.Definition: A circular or elliptical path of one object around another object, particularly in astronomy and space travel.

LAKES.Definition: A small stream of running water; a channel for water; a drain.

SWISS.Definition: To prepare (meat, fabric, etc.) by rolling or pounding in order to soften it.

PRIZE.Definition: That which is taken from another; something captured; a thing seized by force, stratagem, or superior power.

SHELF.Definition: A flat, rigid structure, fixed at right angles to a wall or forming a part of a cabinet, desk etc., and used to support, store or display objects.

RIDGE.Definition: The back of any animal; especially the upper or projecting part of the back of a quadruped.

BOOTS.Definition: A heavy shoe that covers part of the leg.

CIVIC.Definition: Of, relating to, or belonging to a city, a citizen, or citizenship; municipal or civil.

BEANS.Definition: A reusable software component written in Java.

QUERY.Definition: A question, an inquiry (US), an enquiry (UK).

SKULL.Definition: The main bones of the head considered as a unit; including the cranium, facial bones, and mandible.

ADAPT.Definition: To make suitable; to make to correspond; to fit or suit

CARED.Definition: To be concerned (about), to have an interest (in); to feel concern (about).

COINS.Definition: (money) A piece of currency, usually metallic and in the shape of a disc, but sometimes polygonal, or with a hole in the middle.

HONEY.Definition: A viscous, sweet fluid produced from plant nectar by bees. Often used to sweeten tea or to spread on baked goods.

SUITS.Definition: A set of clothes to be worn together, now especially a man's matching jacket and trousers (also business suit or lounge suit), or a similar outfit for a woman.

RISEN.Definition: To move, or appear to move, physically upwards relative to the ground.

FETAL.Definition: Pertaining to, or connected with, a fetus.

VOCAL.Definition: A vocal sound; specifically, a purely vocal element of speech, unmodified except by resonance; a vowel or a diphthong; a tonic element; a tonic; distinguished from a subvocal, and a nonvocal

MAKER.Definition: Someone who makes; a person or thing that makes or produces something.

APPLE.Definition: A common, round fruit produced by the tree Malus domestica, cultivated in temperate climates.

TENTH.Definition: The person or thing coming next after the ninth in a series; that which is in the tenth position.

CHARM.Definition: An object, act or words believed to have magic power (usually carries a positive connotation).

STIFF.Definition: An average person, usually male, of no particular distinction, skill, or education, often a working stiff or lucky stiff.

AMPLE.Definition: Large; great in size, extent, capacity, or bulk; for example spacious, roomy or widely extended.

SHOTS.Definition: The result of launching a projectile or bullet.

JIMMY.Definition: (especially New England and Philadelphia) Chocolate sprinkles used as a topping for ice cream, cookies, or cupcakes.

FATTY.Definition: Containing, composed of, or consisting of fat.

QUASI.Definition: Resembling or having a likeness to something

MASON.Definition: A bricklayer, one whose occupation is to build with stone or brick

DRAIN.Definition: A conduit allowing liquid to flow out of an otherwise contained volume; a plughole (UK)

ELDER.Definition: An older person or an older member, usually a leader, of some community.

MAYOR.Definition: The chief executive of the municipal government of a city, borough, &c., formerly usually appointed as a caretaker by European royal courts but now usually appointed or elected locally.

VAPOR.Definition: Cloudy diffused matter such as mist, steam or fumes suspended in the air.

PAINS.Definition: An ache or bodily suffering, or an instance of this; an unpleasant sensation, resulting from a derangement of functions, disease, or injury by violence; hurt.

WAGON.Definition: A four-wheeled cart for hauling loads.

SWEAT.Definition: Fluid that exits the body through pores in the skin usually due to physical stress and/or high temperature for the purpose of regulating body temperature and removing certain compounds from the circulation.

LIMBS.Definition: A major appendage of human or animal, used for locomotion (such as an arm, leg or wing).

WELLS.Definition: A hole sunk into the ground as a source of water, oil, natural gas or other fluids.

ANGEL.Definition: An incorporeal and sometimes divine messenger from a deity, or other divine entity, often depicted in art as a youthful winged figure in flowing robes.

BALLS.Definition: A solid or hollow sphere, or roughly spherical mass.

GAUGE.Definition: A measure; a standard of measure; an instrument to determine dimensions, distance, or capacity; a standard

VERBS.Definition: (grammar) A word that indicates an action, event, or state.

POLES.Definition: Originally, a stick; now specifically, a long and slender piece of metal or (especially) wood, used for various construction or support purposes.

TRICK.Definition: Something designed to fool or swindle.

WAIST.Definition: The part of the body between the pelvis and the stomach.

POLAR.Definition: The line joining the points of contact of tangents drawn to meet a curve from a point called the pole of the line.

BARRY.Definition: A field divided transversely into several equal parts, and consisting of two different tinctures interchangeably disposed.

ARENA.Definition: An enclosed area, often outdoor, for the presentation of sporting events (sports arena) or other spectacular events; earthen area, often oval, specifically for rodeos (North America) or circular area for bullfights (especially Hispanic America).

BRASS.Definition: A metallic alloy of copper and zinc used in many industrial and plumbing applications.

MILLS.Definition: A grinding apparatus for substances such as grains, seeds, etc.

IRONY.Definition: A statement that, when taken in context, may actually mean something different from, or the opposite of, what is written literally; the use of words expressing something other than their literal intention, often in a humorous context.

LOSES.Definition: To cause (something) to cease to be in one's possession or capability due to unfortunate or unknown circumstances, events or reasons.

BEAMS.Definition: Any large piece of timber or iron long in proportion to its thickness, and prepared for use.

RATED.Definition: To assign or be assigned a particular rank or level.

AUDIO.Definition: A sound, or a sound signal

FLIES.Definition: The open area above a stage where scenery and equipment may be hung.

MYTHS.Definition: A traditional story which embodies a belief regarding some fact or phenomenon of experience, and in which often the forces of nature and of the soul are personified; a sacred narrative regarding a god, a hero, the origin of the world or of a people, etc.

FOLKS.Definition: A grouping of smaller peoples or tribes as a nation.

LACKS.Definition: A defect or failing; moral or spiritual degeneracy.

SNAKE.Definition: A legless reptile of the sub-order Serpentes with a long, thin body and a fork-shaped tongue.

PATCH.Definition: A piece of cloth, or other suitable material, sewed or otherwise fixed upon a garment to repair or strengthen it, especially upon an old garment to cover a hole.

RIFLE.Definition: A shouldered firearm with a long, rifled barrel to improve range and accuracy.

SENDS.Definition: To make something (such as an object or message) go from one place to another.

SAUCE.Definition: A liquid (often thickened) condiment or accompaniment to food.

MONKS.Definition: A male member of a monastic order who has devoted his life for religious service.

PORCH.Definition: A covered and enclosed entrance to a building, whether taken from the interior, and forming a sort of vestibule within the main wall, or projecting without and with a separate roof.

STRAW.Definition: A dried stalk of a cereal plant.

FREED.Definition: To make free; set at liberty; release.

FEAST.Definition: A very large meal, often of a ceremonial nature.

GENUS.Definition: A rank in the classification of organisms, below family and above species; a taxon at that rank.

RUINS.Definition: (sometimes in the plural) The remains of a destroyed or dilapidated construction, such as a house or castle.

CAROL.Definition: A small closet or enclosure built against a window on the inner side, to sit in for study.

SIDED.Definition: To ally oneself, be in an alliance, usually with "with" or rarely "in with"

SPRAY.Definition: A fine, gentle, dispersed mist of liquid.

TRAIT.Definition: An identifying characteristic, habit or trend.

GHOST.Definition: The spirit; the soul of man.

CRIES.Definition: A shedding of tears; the act of crying.

AIDED.Definition: To provide support to; to further the progress of; to help; to assist.

PARKS.Definition: An area of land set aside for environment preservation or recreation.

RITES.Definition: That which complies with justice, law or reason.

CRASH.Definition: A sudden, intense, loud sound, as made for example by cymbals.

TOKEN.Definition: Something serving as an expression of something else.

WIRES.Definition: Metal formed into a thin, even thread, now usually by being drawn through a hole in a steel die.

HOSTS.Definition: One which receives or entertains a guest, socially, commercially, or officially.

SWUNG.Definition: To rotate about an off-centre fixed point.

PETTY.Definition: (usually in the plural) A little schoolboy, either in grade or size.

DRILL.Definition: A tool used to remove material so as to create a hole, typically by plunging a rotating cutting bit into a stationary workpiece.

PERRY.Definition: A fermented alcoholic beverage made from pears; somewhat analogous to cider.

ROBIN.Definition: Any of various passerine birds (about 100 species) of the families Muscicapidae, Turdidae and Petroicidae (formerly Eopsaltriidae), typically with a red breast.
