WRIST.Definition: The complex joint between forearm bones, carpus, and metacarpals where the hand is attached to the arm; the carpus in a narrow sense.

LOCUS.Definition: A place or locality, especially a centre of activity or the scene of a crime.

SEXES.Definition: An offshoot of a larger religion; a group sharing particular (often unorthodox) political and/or religious beliefs.

BREED.Definition: All animals or plants of the same species or subspecies.

BELLY.Definition: The abdomen, especially a fat one.

PIPES.Definition: Meanings relating to a wind instrument.

WELSH.Definition: To swindle someone by not paying a debt, especially a gambling debt.

BACON.Definition: Cured meat from the sides, belly or back of a pig.

TERRY.Definition: A type of coarse cotton fabric covered in many small raised loops that is used to make towels, bathrobes and some types of nappy/diaper.

VIRAL.Definition: A video, image or text spread by "word of mouth" on the internet or by e-mail for humorous, political or marketing purposes.

BEAST.Definition: Any animal other than a human; usually only applied to land vertebrates, especially large or dangerous four-footed ones.

ELBOW.Definition: The joint between the upper arm and the forearm.

SALTS.Definition: A common substance, chemically consisting mainly of sodium chloride (NaCl), used extensively as a condiment and preservative.

BACKS.Definition: The rear of the body, especially the part between the neck and the end of the spine and opposite the chest and belly.

HOMER.Definition: Various former units of volume, particularly:

ALPHA.Definition: The name of the first letter of the Greek alphabet (A,  a), followed by beta. In the Latin alphabet it is the predecessor to A.

FADED.Definition: To grow weak; to lose strength; to decay; to perish gradually; to wither, as a plant.

NASAL.Definition: A medicine that operates through the nose; an errhine.

NINTH.Definition: The person or thing in the ninth position.

MACRO.Definition: Very large in scope or scale.

LORDS.Definition: The master of the servants of a household; the master of a feudal manor

WAVED.Definition: To relinquish (a right etc.); to give up claim to; to forego.

SCREW.Definition: A device that has a helical function.

OLIVE.Definition: A tree, Olea europaea, cultivated since ancient times in the Mediterranean for its fruit and the oil obtained from it.

ROLLS.Definition: The act or result of rolling, or state of being rolled.

BUNCH.Definition: A group of similar things, either growing together, or in a cluster or clump, usually fastened together.

STAMP.Definition: An act of stamping the foot, paw or hoof.

STERN.Definition: Having a hardness and severity of nature or manner.

WEARY.Definition: To make or to become weary.

BURNT.Definition: To cause to be consumed by fire.

PANTS.Definition: (Manchester) An outer garment that covers the body from the waist downwards, covering each leg separately, usually as far as the ankles; trousers.

RADAR.Definition: A method of detecting distant objects and determining their position, velocity, or other characteristics by analysis of sent radio waves (usually microwaves) reflected from their surfaces

BOWEL.Definition: A part or division of the intestines, usually the large intestine.

BARON.Definition: The male ruler of a barony.

KNOCK.Definition: Sudden fatigue as a result of glycogen depletion from not having taken in enough nutrition.

SPERM.Definition: The reproductive cell or gamete of the male; a spermatozoon.

DARED.Definition: To have enough courage (to do something).

AXIAL.Definition: A flight feather that appears between the primaries and secondaries on some birds.

BLEND.Definition: A mixture of two or more things.

PEARL.Definition: A shelly concretion, usually rounded, and having a brilliant luster, with varying tints, found in the mantle, or between the mantle and shell, of certain bivalve mollusks, especially in the pearl oysters and river mussels, and sometimes in certain univalves. It is usually due to a secretion of shelly substance around some irritating foreign particle. Its substance is the same as nacre, or mother-of-pearl. Round lustrous pearls are used in jewellery.

OVERT.Definition: Open and not concealed or secret.

FLOAT.Definition: A buoyant device used to support something in water or another liquid.

TWINS.Definition: Either of two people (or, less commonly, animals) who shared the same uterus at the same time; one who was born at the same birth as a sibling.

ALLOY.Definition: A metal that is a combination of two or more elements, at least one of which is a metal.

CHIPS.Definition: A small piece broken from a larger piece of solid material.

HEELS.Definition: The rear part of the foot, where it joins the leg.

BOMBS.Definition: An explosive device used or intended as a weapon.

LOBBY.Definition: An entryway or reception area; vestibule; passageway; corridor.

CURSE.Definition: A supernatural detriment or hindrance; a bane.

DELTA.Definition: The fourth letter of the modern Greek alphabet Δ, δ.

LODGE.Definition: A building for recreational use such as a hunting lodge or a summer cabin.

WIPED.Definition: To move an object over, maintaining contact, with the intention of removing some substance from the surface. (Compare rub.)

LIPID.Definition: Any of a group of organic compounds including the fats, oils, waxes, sterols, and triglycerides. Lipids are characterized by being insoluble in water, and account for most of the fat present in the human body.

BATCH.Definition: The quantity of bread or other baked goods baked at one time.

OSCAR.Definition: Astronotus ocellatus, a cichlid fish, native to South America, sometimes kept in aquariums.

SEIZE.Definition: To deliberately take hold of; to grab or capture.

UNITE.Definition: A British gold coin worth 20 shillings, first produced during the reign of King James I, and bearing a legend indicating the king's intention of uniting the kingdoms of England and Scotland.

LAMPS.Definition: A device that generates heat, light or other radiation. Especially an electric light bulb.

STEIN.Definition: A beer mug, usually made of ceramic or glass.

NAILS.Definition: The thin, horny plate at the ends of fingers and toes on humans and some other animals.

HARDY.Definition: (usually in the plural) Anything, especially a plant, that is hardy.

BEADS.Definition: Prayer, later especially with a rosary.

REBEL.Definition: A person who resists an established authority, often violently

BURNS.Definition: A physical injury caused by heat, cold, electricity, radiation or caustic chemicals.

DAIRY.Definition: (also dairy products or dairy produce) Products produced from milk.

ERECT.Definition: Upright; vertical or reaching broadly upwards.

YEAST.Definition: An often humid, yellowish froth produced by fermenting malt worts, and used to brew beer, leaven bread, and also used in certain medicines.

CRUST.Definition: A more solid, dense or hard layer on a surface or boundary.

BEARD.Definition: Facial hair on the chin, cheeks, jaw and neck.

DWELL.Definition: A period of time in which a system or component remains in a given state.

GRAFT.Definition: A small shoot or scion of a tree inserted in another tree, the stock of which is to support and nourish it. The two unite and become one tree, but the graft determines the kind of fruit.

FETUS.Definition: An unborn or unhatched vertebrate showing signs of the mature animal.

SWIFT.Definition: A small plain-colored bird of the family Apodidae that resembles a swallow and is noted for its rapid flight.

EVILS.Definition: Moral badness; wickedness; malevolence; the forces or behaviors that are the opposite or enemy of good.

MOTIF.Definition: A recurring or dominant element; a theme.

RESIN.Definition: A viscous hydrocarbon secretion of many plants, particularly coniferous trees.

STOVE.Definition: A heater, a closed apparatus to burn fuel for the warming of a room.

CLUES.Definition: A strand of yarn etc. as used to guide one through a labyrinth; something which points the way, a guide.

POOLS.Definition: A small and rather deep collection of (usually) fresh water, as one supplied by a spring, or occurring in the course of a stream; a reservoir for water.

COUCH.Definition: Couch grass, a species of persistent grass, Elymus repens, usually considered a weed.

DEITY.Definition: A supernatural divine being; a god or goddess.

WOVEN.Definition: To form something by passing lengths or strands of material over and under one another.

LOOPS.Definition: A length of thread, line or rope that is doubled over to make an opening.

SUITE.Definition: A retinue or company of attendants, as of a distinguished personage

CHASE.Definition: The act of one who chases another; a pursuit.

PASTE.Definition: A soft moist mixture, in particular:

MAIZE.Definition: Corn; a type of grain of the species Zea mays.

BOWED.Definition: To play music on (a stringed) instrument using a bow.

SKIRT.Definition: An article of clothing, usually worn by women and girls, that hangs from the waist and covers the lower part of the body.

CREST.Definition: The summit of a hill or mountain ridge.

RANCH.Definition: A large plot of land used for raising cattle, sheep or other livestock.

SINUS.Definition: A pouch or cavity in any organ or tissue, especially the paranasal sinus.

LINEN.Definition: Thread or cloth made from flax fiber.

RABBI.Definition: A Jewish scholar or teacher of halacha (Jewish law), capable of making halachic decisions.

FAIRY.Definition: The realm of faerie; enchantment, illusion.

RENTS.Definition: A payment made by a tenant at intervals in order to occupy a property.

VOWEL.Definition: A sound produced by the vocal cords with relatively little restriction of the oral cavity, forming the prominent sound of a syllable.

HEARS.Definition: (stative) To perceive sounds through the ear.

FIBRE.Definition: A single piece of a given material, elongated and roughly round in cross-section, often twisted with other fibres to form thread.

PIOUS.Definition: Of or pertaining to piety, exhibiting piety, devout, godfearing.

STAYS.Definition: Continuance or a period of time spent in a place; abode for an indefinite time; sojourn.

ROSES.Definition: A shrub of the genus Rosa, with red, pink, white or yellow flowers.

SLICE.Definition: That which is thin and broad.

WEIGH.Definition: To determine the weight of an object.

PURSE.Definition: A small bag for carrying money.

PAGAN.Definition: A person not adhering to a main world religion; a follower of a pantheistic or nature-worshipping religion.

FLUNG.Definition: To move (oneself) abruptly or violently; to rush or dash.

HEIRS.Definition: Someone who inherits, or is designated to inherit, the property of another.

DIGIT.Definition: A stage of proficiency or qualification in a course of study, now especially an award bestowed by a university or, in some countries, a college, as a certification of academic achievement. (In the United States, can include secondary schools.)

SWEAR.Definition: To take an oath, to promise.

ASHES.Definition: The solid remains of a fire.

EDEMA.Definition: An excessive accumulation of serum in tissue spaces or a body cavity.

JENNY.Definition: A device for spinning thread from fiber onto multiple spindles (also called spinning jenny).

BELLS.Definition: A percussive instrument made of metal or other hard material, typically but not always in the shape of an inverted cup with a flared rim, which resonates when struck.

ETHER.Definition: The substance formerly supposed to fill the upper regions of the atmosphere above the clouds, in particular as a medium breathed by deities.

SHOUT.Definition: A loud burst of voice or voices; a violent and sudden outcry, especially that of a multitude expressing joy, triumph, exultation, anger, or animated courage.

MONTE.Definition: A game in which three or four cards are dealt face-up and players bet on which of them will first be matched in suit by others dealt.

HERBS.Definition: Any green, leafy plant, or parts thereof, used to flavour or season food.

TOMMY.Definition: Bread, generally a penny roll; the supply of food carried by workmen as their daily allowance

NAZIS.Definition: A member of the National Socialist German Workers Party (Nationalsozialistische Deutsche Arbeiterpartei, commonly called the NSDAP or Nazi Party).

DREAD.Definition: Great fear in view of impending evil; fearful apprehension of danger; anticipatory terror.

GAMMA.Definition: The third letter of the Greek alphabet (Γ, γ), preceded by beta (Β, β) and followed by delta, (Δ, δ).

CANON.Definition: A generally accepted principle; a rule.

ALBUM.Definition: A book specially designed to keep photographs, stamps, or autographs.

SALAD.Definition: A food made primarily of a mixture of raw or cold ingredients, typically vegetables, usually served with a dressing such as vinegar or mayonnaise.

HORNS.Definition: A hard growth of keratin that protrudes from the top of the head of certain animals, usually paired.

PALMS.Definition: Any of various evergreen trees from the family Palmae or Arecaceae, which are mainly found in the tropics.

PUMPS.Definition: A device for moving or compressing a liquid or gas.

TAPES.Definition: Flexible material in a roll with a sticky surface on one or both sides; adhesive tape.

SPARK.Definition: A small particle of glowing matter, either molten or on fire.

GRAMS.Definition: A photograph or video shared on this service.

FILLS.Definition: To occupy fully, to take up all of.

CLIFF.Definition: A vertical (or nearly vertical) rock face.

PUNCH.Definition: A hit or strike with one's fist.

IONIC.Definition: Of, relating to, or containing ions

WITCH.Definition: A person who practices witchcraft; a woman or man who practices witchcraft.

TOURS.Definition: A journey through a particular building, estate, country, etc.

BONUS.Definition: Something extra that is good; an added benefit.

PENIS.Definition: The male reproductive organ used for sexual intercourse that in the human male and some other mammals is also used for urination; the tubular portion of the male genitalia (excluding the scrotum).

MODEM.Definition: A device that encodes digital computer signals into analog/analogue telephone signals and vice versa and allows computers to communicate over a phone line.

WEEDS.Definition: Any plant regarded as unwanted at the place where, and at the time when it is growing.

RIOTS.Definition: Wanton or unrestrained behavior; uproar; tumult.

SOBER.Definition: (often with up) To make or become sober.

ANKLE.Definition: The skeletal joint which connects the foot with the leg; the uppermost portion of the foot and lowermost portion of the leg, which contain this skeletal joint.

SAFER.Definition: Not in danger; out of harm's reach.

BUSES.Definition: A motor vehicle for transporting large numbers of people along roads.

ONION.Definition: A monocotyledonous plant (Allium cepa), allied to garlic, used as vegetable and spice.

NOISY.Definition: Making a noise, especially a loud unpleasant sound

TROUT.Definition: Any of several species of fish in Salmonidae, closely related to salmon, and distinguished by spawning more than once.

FLOCK.Definition: A large number of birds, especially those gathered together for the purpose of migration.

MASKS.Definition: A cover, or partial cover, for the face, used for disguise or protection.

SCOTS.Definition: A local tax, paid originally to the lord or ruler and later to a sheriff.

SETUP.Definition: Equipment designed for a particular purpose; an apparatus.

INFER.Definition: To introduce (something) as a reasoned conclusion; to conclude by reasoning or deduction, as from premises or evidence.

FUNGI.Definition: Any member of the kingdom Fungi; a eukaryotic organism typically having chitin cell walls but no chlorophyll or plastids. Fungi may be unicellular or multicellular.

CREEP.Definition: To move slowly with the abdomen close to the ground.

COUGH.Definition: A sudden, usually noisy expulsion of air from the lungs, often involuntary.

ABBEY.Definition: The office or dominion of an abbot or abbess.

DRUMS.Definition: A percussive musical instrument spanned with a thin covering on at least one end for striking, forming an acoustic chamber, affecting what materials are used to make it; a membranophone.

ROBOT.Definition: A machine built to carry out some complex task or group of tasks by physically moving, especially one which can be programmed.

QUOTA.Definition: A proportional part or share; the share or proportion assigned to each in a division.

NOUNS.Definition: (grammar, narrow sense) A word that can be used to refer to a person, animal, place, thing, phenomenon, substance, quality, or idea; one of the basic parts of speech in many languages, including English.

MOLLY.Definition: A woman or girl, especially of low status.

FLANK.Definition: The flesh between the last rib and the hip; the side.

EAGLE.Definition: Any of several large carnivorous and carrion-eating birds in the family Accipitridae, having a powerful hooked bill and keen vision.

FUZZY.Definition: (often in the plural) A very small piece of plush material.

WEARS.Definition: To carry or have equipped on or about one's body, as an item of clothing, equipment, decoration, etc.

BLESS.Definition: To make something holy by religious rite, sanctify.

ARBOR.Definition: A shady sitting place or pergola usually in a park or garden, surrounded by climbing shrubs, vines or other vegetation.

CLASH.Definition: A loud sound, like the crashing together of metal objects.

FLAGS.Definition: A piece of cloth, often decorated with an emblem, used as a visual signal or symbol.

SHINE.Definition: Brightness from a source of light.

RAINS.Definition: Condensed water falling from a cloud.

FUELS.Definition: Substance consumed to provide energy through combustion, or through chemical or nuclear reaction.

SKINS.Definition: The outer protective layer of the body of any animal, including of a human.

EPOCH.Definition: A particular period of history, especially one considered noteworthy or remarkable.

COATS.Definition: An outer garment covering the upper torso and arms.Wp

STOOL.Definition: A seat, especially for one person and without armrests.

CITES.Definition: To quote; to repeat, as a passage from a book, or the words of another.

SEALS.Definition: A pinniped (Pinnipedia), particularly an earless seal (true seal) or eared seal.

CREWS.Definition: A pen for livestock such as chickens or pigs

RAIDS.Definition: A quick hostile or predatory incursion or invasion in a battle.

MARSH.Definition: An area of low, wet land, often with tall grass.

TAXED.Definition: To impose and collect a tax from (a person or company).

CANOE.Definition: A small long and narrow boat, propelled by one or more people (depending on the size of canoe), using single-bladed paddles. The paddlers face in the direction of travel, in either a seated position, or kneeling on the bottom of the boat. Canoes are open on top, and pointed at both ends.

DIETS.Definition: The food and beverage a person or animal consumes.

PILES.Definition: A mass of things heaped together; a heap.

ETHIC.Definition: A set of principles of right and wrong behaviour guiding, or representative of, a specific culture, society, group, or individual.

TRAPS.Definition: A machine or other device designed to catch (and sometimes kill) animals, either by holding them in a container, or by catching hold of part of the body.

BAKED.Definition: (with person as subject) To cook (something) in an oven.

HAIRS.Definition: A pigmented filament of keratin which grows from a follicle on the skin of humans and other mammals.

THIGH.Definition: The upper leg of a human, between the hip and the knee.

CHOIR.Definition: Singing group; group of people who sing together; company of people who are trained to sing together.

DISKS.Definition: A thin, flat, circular plate or similar object.

SURGE.Definition: A sudden transient rush, flood or increase.

BERRY.Definition: A small succulent fruit, of any one of many varieties.

BLUES.Definition: The colour of the clear sky or the deep sea, between green and violet in the visible spectrum, and one of the primary additive colours for transmitted light; the colour obtained by subtracting red and green from white light using magenta and cyan filters; or any colour resembling this.

WINES.Definition: An alcoholic beverage made by fermenting the juice of grapes.

CANDY.Definition: Edible, sweet-tasting confectionery containing sugar, or sometimes artificial sweeteners, and often flavored with fruit, chocolate, nuts, herbs and spices, or artificial flavors.

LOFTY.Definition: High, tall, having great height or stature

LADEN.Definition: To fill or load (related to cargo or a shipment).

TOWEL.Definition: A cloth used for wiping, especially one used for drying anything wet, as a person after a bath.

ALGAE.Definition: Any of many aquatic photosynthetic organisms, including the seaweeds, whose size ranges from a single cell to giant kelps and whose biochemistry and forms are very diverse, some being eukaryotic.

CLOAK.Definition: A long outer garment worn over the shoulders covering the back; a cape, often with a hood.

JOINS.Definition: An intersection of piping or wiring; an interconnect.

CHORD.Definition: A harmonic set of three or more notes that is heard as if sounding simultaneously.

HEATH.Definition: A tract of level uncultivated land with sandy soil and scrubby vegetation; heathland.

STOLE.Definition: To take illegally, or without the owner's permission, something owned by someone else.

ATLAS.Definition: A bound collection of maps often including tables, illustrations or other text.

WHALE.Definition: Any of several species of large sea mammals of the infraorder Cetacea.

CREED.Definition: That which is believed; accepted doctrine, especially religious doctrine; a particular set of beliefs; any summary of principles or opinions professed or adhered to.

WEDGE.Definition: One of the simple machines; a piece of material, such as metal or wood, thick at one edge and tapered to a thin edge at the other for insertion in a narrow crevice, used for splitting, tightening, securing, or levering.

AVAIL.Definition: Effect in achieving a goal or aim; purpose, use (now usually in negative constructions).

FLUSH.Definition: A group of birds that have suddenly started up from undergrowth, trees etc.

PROXY.Definition: An agent or substitute authorized to act for another person.

CHILL.Definition: A moderate, but uncomfortable and penetrating coldness.

UNDUE.Definition: Excessive; going beyond that what is natural or sufficient.

HEDGE.Definition: A thicket of bushes or other shrubbery, especially one planted as a fence between two portions of land, or to separate the parts of a garden.

TYPED.Definition: To put text on paper using a typewriter.

MERGE.Definition: The joining together of multiple sources.

CORAL.Definition: A hard substance made of the limestone skeletons of marine polyps.

POLLS.Definition: A survey of people, usually statistically analyzed to gauge wider public opinion.

GOATS.Definition: A mammal, Capra aegagrus hircus, and similar species of the genus Capra.

BOOST.Definition: A push from behind, as to one who is endeavoring to climb.

DITCH.Definition: A trench; a long, shallow indentation, as for irrigation or drainage.

LEVER.Definition: (except in generalized senses below) A crowbar.

PILED.Definition: (often used with the preposition "up") To lay or throw into a pile or heap; to heap up; to collect into a mass; to accumulate

LOCKS.Definition: Something used for fastening, which can only be opened with a key or combination.

MOUND.Definition: An artificial hill or elevation of earth; a raised bank; an embankment thrown up for defense

DOUGH.Definition: A thick, malleable substance made by mixing flour with other ingredients such as water, eggs, and/or butter, that is made into a particular form and then baked.

FROST.Definition: A cover of minute ice crystals on objects that are exposed to the air. Frost is formed by the same process as dew, except that the temperature of the frosted object is below freezing.

IVORY.Definition: The hard white form of dentin which forms the tusks of elephants, walruses and other animals.

SCRAP.Definition: A (small) piece; a fragment; a detached, incomplete portion.

VILLA.Definition: (plural "villas") A house, often larger and more expensive than average, in the countryside or on the coast, often used as a retreat.

PSALM.Definition: A sacred song; a poetical composition for use in the praise or worship of God.

BLOOM.Definition: A blossom; the flower of a plant; an expanded bud.

ROOFS.Definition: The external covering at the top of a building.

RALLY.Definition: A public gathering or mass meeting that is not mainly a protest and is organized to inspire enthusiasm for a cause.

KNOTS.Definition: A looping of a piece of string or of any other long, flexible material that cannot be untangled without passing one or both ends of the material through its loops.

FUSED.Definition: To melt together; to blend; to mix indistinguishably.

GREED.Definition: A selfish or excessive desire for more than is needed or deserved, especially of money, wealth, food, or other possessions.

THIEF.Definition: One who carries out a theft.

EXCEL.Definition: To surpass someone or something; to be better or do better than someone or something.

OUNCE.Definition: An avoirdupois ounce, weighing 1/16 of an avoirdupois pound, or 28.3495 grams.

CRANE.Definition: Any bird of the family Gruidae, large birds with long legs and a long neck which is extended during flight.

BLOND.Definition: A pale yellowish (golden brown) color, especially said of hair color.

SWORN.Definition: To take an oath, to promise.

PENNY.Definition: In the United Kingdom and Ireland, a copper coin worth 1/240 of a pound sterling or Irish pound before decimalisation. Abbreviation: d.

WORMS.Definition: A generally tubular invertebrate of the annelid phylum; an earthworm.

MOLAR.Definition: A back tooth having a broad surface used for grinding one's food.

FETCH.Definition: An act of fetching, of bringing something from a distance.

TAILS.Definition: The caudal appendage of an animal that is attached to its posterior and near the anus.

OZONE.Definition: An allotrope of oxygen (symbol O₃) having three atoms in the molecule instead of the usual two; it is a blue gas, generated from oxygen by electrical discharge.

SPOON.Definition: An implement for eating or serving; a scooped utensil whose long handle is straight, in contrast to a ladle.

ROTOR.Definition: A rotating part of a mechanical device, for example in an electric motor, generator, alternator or pump.

CAVES.Definition: A large, naturally-occurring cavity formed underground or in the face of a cliff or a hillside.

BOOTH.Definition: A small stall for the display and sale of goods.

BLUNT.Definition: A fencer's practice foil with a soft tip.

TENTS.Definition: A pavilion or portable lodge consisting of skins, canvas, or some strong cloth, stretched and sustained by poles, used for sheltering people from the weather.

BYTES.Definition: A short sequence of bits (binary digits) that can be operated on as a unit by a computer; the smallest usable machine word.

PAVED.Definition: To cover something with paving slabs.

RIDER.Definition: One who rides, often on a horse or a motorcycle.

CRUSH.Definition: A violent collision or compression; a crash; destruction; ruin.

CACHE.Definition: A store of things that may be required in the future, which can be retrieved rapidly, protected or hidden in some way.

NICHE.Definition: A cavity, hollow, or recess, generally within the thickness of a wall, for a statue, bust, or other erect ornament. Hence, any similar position, literal or figurative.

EDGED.Definition: To move an object slowly and carefully in a particular direction.

WILLS.Definition: One's independent faculty of choice; the ability to be able to exercise one's choice or intention.

LYNCH.Definition: To execute (somebody) without a proper legal trial or procedure, especially by hanging and backed by a mob.

ULCER.Definition: An open sore of the skin, eyes or mucous membrane, often caused by an initial abrasion and generally maintained by an inflammation and/or an infection.

TROOP.Definition: (collective) A collection of people; a number; a multitude (in general).

SWELL.Definition: To become bigger, especially due to being engorged.

CREPT.Definition: To move slowly with the abdomen close to the ground.

PAPAL.Definition: Having to do with the pope or the papacy.

FAUNA.Definition: Animals considered as a group; especially those of a particular country, region, time.

BOSOM.Definition: (somewhat obsolete) The breast or chest of a human (or sometimes of another animal).

GREET.Definition: To welcome in a friendly manner, either in person or through another means e.g. writing or over the phone/internet

EMAIL.Definition: A system for transferring messages from one computer to another, usually through a network.

TILES.Definition: A regularly-shaped slab of clay or other material, affixed to cover or decorate a surface, as in a roof-tile, glazed tile, stove tile, carpet tile etc.

QUEER.Definition: (sometimes derogatory) A person who is or appears homosexual, or who has homosexual qualities.

HIDES.Definition: To put (something) in a place where it will be harder to discover or out of sight.

FLORA.Definition: Plants considered as a group, especially those of a particular country, region, time, etc.

PILLS.Definition: A small, usually round or cylindrical object designed for easy swallowing, usually containing some sort of medication.

BELTS.Definition: A band worn around the waist to hold clothing to one's body (usually pants), hold weapons (such as a gun or sword), or serve as a decorative piece of clothing.

CLUNG.Definition: To hold very tightly, as to not fall off.

EVOKE.Definition: To call out; to draw out or bring forth.

TRASH.Definition: Useless things to be discarded; rubbish; refuse.

DIODE.Definition: An electronic device that allows current to flow in one direction only; used chiefly as a rectifier.

ARMOR.Definition: A protective layer over a body, vehicle, or other object intended to deflect or diffuse damaging forces.

INERT.Definition: A substance that does not react chemically.

RENEW.Definition: To make (something) new again; to restore to freshness or original condition.

HERDS.Definition: A number of domestic animals assembled together under the watch or ownership of a keeper.

TIGER.Definition: Panthera tigris, a large predatory mammal of the cat family, indigenous to Asia.

SKIES.Definition: The atmosphere above a given point, especially as visible from the ground during the day.

AVANT.Definition: The front of an army; the vanguard.

BATHS.Definition: A tub or pool which is used for bathing: bathtub.

TUTOR.Definition: One who teaches another (usually called a student, learner, or tutee) in a one-on-one or small-group interaction.

HYMNS.Definition: A song of praise or worship.

SANDS.Definition: Rock that is ground more finely than gravel, but is not as fine as silt (more formally, see grain sizes chart), forming beaches and deserts and also used in construction.

PICKS.Definition: A tool used for digging; a pickaxe.

BOLTS.Definition: A (usually) metal fastener consisting of a cylindrical body that is threaded, with a larger head on one end. It can be inserted into an unthreaded hole up to the head, with a nut then threaded on the other end; a heavy machine screw.

SPEAR.Definition: A long stick with a sharp tip used as a weapon for throwing or thrusting, or anything used to make a thrusting motion.

OPIUM.Definition: A yellow-brown, addictive narcotic drug obtained from the dried juice of unripe pods of the opium poppy, Papaver somniferum, and containing alkaloids such as morphine, codeine, and papaverine.

VIGOR.Definition: Active strength or force of body or mind; capacity for exertion, physically, intellectually, or morally; energy.

MOULD.Definition: A hollow form or matrix for shaping a fluid or plastic substance.

NESTS.Definition: A structure built by a bird as a place to incubate eggs and rear young.

BITCH.Definition: (dog-breeding) A female dog or other canine, particularly a recent mother.

AORTA.Definition: The great artery which carries the blood from the heart to all parts of the body except the lungs; the main trunk of the arterial system.

EASED.Definition: To free (something) from pain, worry, agitation, etc.

ROPES.Definition: Thick strings, yarn, monofilaments, metal wires, or strands of other cordage that are twisted together to form a stronger line.

TUNED.Definition: To adjust (a musical instrument) so that it produces the correct pitches.

REVUE.Definition: A form of theatrical entertainment in which recent events, popular fads, etc., are parodied. Any entertainment featuring skits, dances, and songs.

TOMBS.Definition: A small building (or "vault") for the remains of the dead, with walls, a roof, and (if it is to be used for more than one corpse) a door. It may be partly or wholly in the ground (except for its entrance) in a cemetery, or it may be inside a church proper or in its crypt. Single tombs may be permanently sealed; those for families (or other groups) have doors for access whenever needed.

URGES.Definition: A strong desire; an itch to do something.

CAKES.Definition: A rich, sweet dessert food, typically made of flour, sugar and eggs and baked in an oven, and often covered in icing.

MOODS.Definition: A mental or emotional state, composure.

WATTS.Definition: In the International System of Units, the derived unit of power; the power of a system in which one joule of energy is transferred per second. Symbol: W

BOWLS.Definition: A roughly hemispherical container used to hold, mix or present food, such as salad, fruit or soup, or other items.

RACED.Definition: To take part in a race (in the sense of a contest).

ABIDE.Definition: To endure without yielding; to withstand; await defiantly; to encounter; to persevere.

TENOR.Definition: A musical range or section higher than bass and lower than alto.

CHEER.Definition: A cheerful attitude; happiness; a good, happy, or positive mood.

SAILS.Definition: A piece of fabric attached to a boat and arranged such that it causes the wind to drive the boat along. The sail may be attached to the boat via a combination of mast, spars and ropes.

RAILS.Definition: A horizontal bar extending between supports and used for support or as a barrier; a railing.

FERRY.Definition: A ship used to transport people, smaller vehicles and goods from one port to another, usually on a regular schedule.

VEGAS.Definition: (Latin America) An open tract of ground; a plain, especially one which is moist and fertile, such as those used for growing tobacco.

AMEND.Definition: (usually in the plural) An act of righting a wrong; compensation.

CIGAR.Definition: Tobacco rolled and wrapped with an outer covering of tobacco leaves, intended to be smoked.

LUNAR.Definition: The middle bone of the proximal series of the carpus in the wrist, which is shaped like a half-moon.

SQUAD.Definition: A group of people organized for some common purpose, usually of about ten members.

SWAMP.Definition: A piece of wet, spongy land; low ground saturated with water; soft, wet ground which may have a growth of certain kinds of trees, but is unfit for agricultural or pastoral purposes.

PORES.Definition: A tiny opening in the skin.

ALLEY.Definition: A narrow street or passageway, especially one through the middle of a block giving access to the rear of lots or buildings.

LIONS.Definition: A big cat, Panthera leo, native to Africa, India and formerly much of Europe.

BOAST.Definition: A brag; ostentatious positive appraisal of oneself.

PERIL.Definition: A situation of serious and immediate danger.

DENIS.Definition: A subdivision of currency, equal to 0.01 Macedonian denar.

ETHOS.Definition: The character or fundamental values of a person, people, culture, or movement.

DOGMA.Definition: An authoritative principle, belief or statement of opinion, especially one considered to be absolutely true and indisputable, regardless of evidence or without evidence to support it.

WRECK.Definition: Something or someone that has been ruined.

SWORE.Definition: To take an oath, to promise.

ROBES.Definition: A long loose outer garment, often signifying honorary stature.

PONDS.Definition: An inland body of standing water, either natural or man-made, that is smaller than a lake.

VERGE.Definition: A rod or staff of office, e.g. of a verger.

SLIPS.Definition: A thin, slippery mix of clay and water.

SAVES.Definition: In various sports, a block that prevents an opponent from scoring.

PIXEL.Definition: One of the tiny dots that make up the representation of an image in a computer's memory.

DONNA.Definition: A lady, especially a noblewoman; the title given to a lady in Italy.

CORES.Definition: Various former units of volume, particularly:

MERRY.Definition: Jolly and full of high spirits.

VOLTS.Definition: In the International System of Units, the derived unit of electrical potential and electromotive force (voltage); the potential difference across a conductor when a current of one ampere uses one watt of power. Symbol: V

GENET.Definition: Any of several Old World nocturnal, carnivorous mammals, of the genus Genetta in the family Viverridae, most of which have a spotted coat and a long, ringed tail.

DUCTS.Definition: A pipe, tube or canal which carries gas or liquid from one place to another.

STOUT.Definition: A dark and strong malt brew made with toasted grain.

CRISP.Definition: A thin slice of fried potato eaten as a snack.

NIECE.Definition: A daughter of one’s sibling, brother-in-law, or sister-in-law; either the daughter of one's brother ("fraternal niece"), or of one's sister ("sororal niece").

COILS.Definition: Something wound in the form of a helix or spiral.

RAVEN.Definition: Any of several, generally large and lustrous black species of birds in the genus Corvus, especially the common raven, Corvus corax.

SECTS.Definition: An offshoot of a larger religion; a group sharing particular (often unorthodox) political and/or religious beliefs.

SINGS.Definition: A gathering at which people sing songs.

BATES.Definition: To reduce the force of something; to abate.

AISLE.Definition: A wing of a building, notably in a church separated from the nave proper by piers.

VAULT.Definition: An arched masonry structure supporting and forming a ceiling, whether freestanding or forming part of a larger building.

STRAY.Definition: Any domestic animal that has no enclosure, or its proper place and company, and wanders at large, or is lost; an estray.

DUMMY.Definition: A silent person; a person who does not talk.

LITER.Definition: The metric unit of fluid measure, equal to one cubic decimetre. Symbols: l, L, ℓ

FLASK.Definition: A narrow-necked vessel of metal or glass, used for various purposes; as of sheet metal, to carry gunpowder in; or of wrought iron, to contain quicksilver; or of glass, to heat water in, etc.

MORSE.Definition: A clasp or fastening used to fasten a cope in the front, usually decorative.

LENDS.Definition: To allow to be used by someone temporarily, on condition that it or its equivalent will be returned.

AWOKE.Definition: To become conscious after having slept.

PLEAD.Definition: To present (an argument or a plea), especially in a legal case.

SCARS.Definition: A permanent mark on the skin, sometimes caused by the healing of a wound.

WARDS.Definition: A warden; a guard; a guardian or watchman.

SOCKS.Definition: A knitted or woven covering for the foot.

TORCH.Definition: A stick with a flame on one end, used chiefly as a light source; a similarly shaped implement with a replaceable supply of flammable material.

TONIC.Definition: A substance with medicinal properties intended to restore or invigorate.

STALL.Definition: A compartment for a single animal in a stable or cattle shed.

LUMEN.Definition: In the International System of Units, the derived unit of luminous flux; the light that is emitted in a solid angle of one steradian from a source of one candela. Symbol: lm.

GROOM.Definition: A man who is about to marry.

LOBES.Definition: Any projection or division, especially one of a somewhat rounded form.

ROUGE.Definition: Red or pink makeup to add colour to the cheeks; blusher.

DWARF.Definition: Any member of a race of beings from (especially Scandinavian and other Germanic) folklore, usually depicted as having some sort of supernatural powers and being skilled in crafting and metalworking, often as short with long beards, and sometimes as clashing with elves.

ICONS.Definition: An image, symbol, picture, or other representation usually as an object of religious devotion.

TIDES.Definition: The periodic change of the sea level, particularly when caused by the gravitational influence of the sun and the moon.

TOXIN.Definition: A toxic or poisonous substance produced by the biological processes of biological organisms.

PEGGY.Definition: Any of several small warblers, the whitethroat, etc.

SLATE.Definition: A fine-grained homogeneous sedimentary rock composed of clay or volcanic ash which has been metamorphosed so that it cleaves easily into thin layers.

CHESS.Definition: A board game for two players with each beginning with sixteen chess pieces moving according to fixed rules across a chessboard with the objective to checkmate the opposing king.

SCALP.Definition: The top of the head; the skull.

HOOKS.Definition: A rod bent into a curved shape, typically with one end free and the other end secured to a rope or other attachment.

LEVIN.Definition: Lightning; a bolt of lightning; also, a bright flame or light.

CORDS.Definition: A harmonic set of three or more notes that is heard as if sounding simultaneously.

TYING.Definition: To twist (a string, rope, or the like) around itself securely.

KNELT.Definition: To rest on one's bent knees, sometimes only one; to move to such a position.

CLEFT.Definition: An opening, fissure, or V-shaped indentation made by or as if by splitting.

SHALT.Definition: (modal, auxiliary verb, defective) Used before a verb to indicate the simple future tense in the first person singular or plural.

DISCS.Definition: A thin, flat, circular plate or similar object.

GUISE.Definition: Customary way of speaking or acting; fashion, manner, practice (often used formerly in such phrases as "at his own guise"; that is, in his own fashion, to suit himself.)

VINES.Definition: The climbing plant that produces grapes.

SPOIL.Definition: (Also in plural: spoils) Plunder taken from an enemy or victim.

FACET.Definition: Any one of the flat surfaces cut into a gem.

CYSTS.Definition: A pouch or sac without opening, usually membranous and containing morbid matter, which develops in one of the natural cavities or in the substance of an organ.

LEONE.Definition: A unit of currency of Sierra Leone, divided into 100 cents.

DUCKS.Definition: To quickly lower the head or body in order to prevent it from being struck by something.

CRAWL.Definition: The act of moving slowly on hands and knees etc, or with frequent stops.

SLACK.Definition: The part of anything that hangs loose, having no strain upon it.

ANODE.Definition: An electrode, of a cell or other electrically polarized device, through which a positive current of electricity flows inwards (and thus, electrons flow outwards). It can have either a negative or a positive voltage.

GUILD.Definition: A group or association mainly of tradespeople made up of merchants, craftspeople, or artisans for mutual aid, particularly in the Middle Ages.

MATES.Definition: The conclusive victory in a game of chess that occurs when an opponent's king is threatened with unavoidable capture.

HASTY.Definition: Acting in haste; being too hurried or quick

CONES.Definition: A surface of revolution formed by rotating a segment of a line around another line that intersects the first line.

MOTTO.Definition: A sentence, phrase, or word, forming part of an heraldic achievement.

HATCH.Definition: A horizontal door in a floor or ceiling.

TACIT.Definition: Expressed in silence; implied, but not made explicit; silent.

LANES.Definition: (used in street names) A road, street, or similar thoroughfare.

METRE.Definition: To measure with a metering device.

FOOLS.Definition: A person with poor judgment or little intelligence.

FLUTE.Definition: A woodwind instrument consisting of a tube with a row of holes that produce sound through vibrations caused by air blown across the edge of the holes, often tuned by plugging one or more holes with a finger; the Western concert flute, a transverse side-blown flute of European origin.

STEER.Definition: A suggestion about a course of action.

WEAVE.Definition: A type or way of weaving.

FRAIL.Definition: A basket made of rushes, used chiefly to hold figs and raisins.

SPILL.Definition: A mess of something that has been dropped.

JUMPS.Definition: The act of jumping; a leap; a spring; a bound.

HURTS.Definition: An emotional or psychological humiliation or bad experience.

LIFTS.Definition: An act of lifting or raising.

IDIOM.Definition: A manner of speaking, a mode of expression peculiar to a language, person, or group of people.

FISTS.Definition: A hand with the fingers clenched or curled inward.

COCOA.Definition: The dried and partially fermented fatty seeds of the cacao tree from which chocolate is made.

ISLES.Definition: A wing of a building, notably in a church separated from the nave proper by piers.

BROOK.Definition: To use; enjoy; have the full employment of.

CLING.Definition: Fruit (especially peach) whose flesh adheres strongly to the pit.

BULBS.Definition: Any solid object rounded at one end and tapering on the other, possibly attached to a larger object at the tapered end.

SEARS.Definition: To char, scorch, or burn the surface of (something) with a hot instrument.

FORTS.Definition: A fortified defensive structure stationed with troops.

PINCH.Definition: The action of squeezing a small amount of a person's skin and flesh, making it hurt.

ABODE.Definition: To endure without yielding; to withstand; await defiantly; to encounter; to persevere.

REINS.Definition: A strap or rope attached to a bridle or bit, used to control a horse, animal or young child.

HUMID.Definition: Containing perceptible moisture (usually describing air or atmosphere); damp; moist; somewhat wet or watery.

HOLLY.Definition: Any of various shrubs or (mostly) small trees, of the genus Ilex, either evergreen or deciduous, used as decoration especially at Christmas.

WARES.Definition: (usually in combination) Goods or a type of goods offered for sale or use.

MENUS.Definition: The details of the food to be served at a banquet; a bill of fare.

LINUX.Definition: Any unix-like operating system distro that uses the Linux kernel.

CHUCK.Definition: Meat from the shoulder of a cow or other animal.

WISER.Definition: Showing good judgement or the benefit of experience.

PACKS.Definition: A bundle made up and prepared to be carried; especially, a bundle to be carried on the back, but also a load for an animal, a bale.

SYRUP.Definition: Any thick liquid that has a high sugar content and which is added to or poured over food as a flavouring.

OVARY.Definition: A female reproductive organ, often paired, that produces ova and in mammals secretes the hormones estrogen and progesterone.

SLABS.Definition: A large, flat piece of solid material; a solid object that is large and flat.

GRAPE.Definition: A small, round, smooth-skinned edible fruit, usually purple, red, or green, that grows in bunches on vines of genus Vitis.

SPIES.Definition: A person who secretly watches and examines the actions of other individuals or organizations and gathers information on them (usually to gain an advantage).

BRUTE.Definition: An animal seen as being without human reason; a senseless beast.

BROTH.Definition: Water in which food (meat or vegetable etc) has been boiled.

UNFIT.Definition: To make unfit; to render unsuitable, spoil, disqualify.

CLANS.Definition: A group of people all descended from a common ancestor, in fact or belief.

WARNS.Definition: To make (someone) aware of (something impending); especially:

SLASH.Definition: A slashing action or motion, particularly:

The exact seat of hamza is governed by an orthographic rule - "seat of hamza rule".
